# PLAN.md ‚Äî Implementation Roadmap and Status

**This file is the single source of implementation status.** It tells every session
what is done, in progress, blocked, and next. It **references** the
specification in `docs/architecture/` (rule R-4, AGENTS.md) and never restates it:
if you need protocol content, follow the *Spec* column.

How to work this file (full protocol: AGENTS.md):

- Sessions pick **one** milestone: the üî® one, else the first ‚¨ú whose *Depends* are ‚úÖ.
- A milestone is ‚úÖ only with green quality gates and a `spec-reviewer` pass without
  blockers (its *Verify* obligations are part of the milestone).
- Update this file **in the same session as the change**: status, *Current focus*,
  and a *Session log* row. Append to logs; never rewrite their history. Dates absolute
  (YYYY-MM-DD).

Legend: ‚¨ú pending ¬∑ üî® in progress ¬∑ ‚úÖ done ¬∑ ‚õî blocked ¬∑ üÖø deferred (post-v1)

---

## Current focus

**Batch C (integration contract v6 ‚Äî the single contract bump) ‚úÖ CLOSED 2026-07-21 ‚Äî all 32 rows disposed; open 131 ‚Üí 102.** 29 resolved, 3 ruled and reclassified to X. Landed as **`INTEGRATION_CONTRACT_VERSION = 6`** with joint backend+frontend sign-off (the user, owner for both sides under R-1). The batch is the strongest evidence yet for verification-before-amendment: seven parallel Codex agents checked every row against the code first, and **18 of the 32 rows did not survive contact with it**. **Six were already resolved by contract v4 and had simply never been closed out** (SQ-37, SQ-43, SQ-55, SQ-87, SQ-138, SQ-198) ‚Äî a fifth of the batch was phantom work. **Twelve more carried false or stale load-bearing claims**, each corrected before a word of spec text was written: SQ-212's "300√ó" fee error is really **100,000√ó** (raw `Perbill` is parts-per-billion, not bps); SQ-106's entire residual is false ‚Äî FRAME's `BoundedVec` renders in real runtime metadata as a *composite* exactly like the primitives type, so the composite-vs-sequence `TypeInfo` decision it asked for does not exist; SQ-217 names two metadata constants that are not constants (`ReviewDeadlineEpochs` is a live `Config::Get` seam, `AttestorBond` does not exist) and misses the one that is (`PlaybookFreezeWindowBlocks`) ‚Äî 16 omissions, not ~17; SQ-210's claim that an out-of-domain book yields the zero sentinel is false (only a computation error does); and SQ-134's "permanent / no writer ever clears" is persistent-until-next-write. **Two findings the rows understated, both now the batch's real value.** **SQ-134** ‚Äî 02 ¬ß12 claims a compromised release-channel writer can only raise a false "update available" banner. Untrue: all eight combinations of (guard `PendingUpgrade`, channel `pending_authorized_at`, `URGENT_UPGRADE`) were reachable, including **suppressing a live pending upgrade**, which defeats the D-14 descriptor-lead-time warning outright. Root cause was a genuine 02 ¬ß12 self-contradiction ‚Äî offset 112 called "current runtime spec_version" in the field table and written as the "target" one paragraph later. Ruled to **currently installed, always**, which is the direction a stranded metadata-less reader needs and which dissolves the stale-after-abort problem instead of needing a restore path; guard ownership of offsets 112‚Äì119 and flag bit 2 is now normative, `set_release_channel` merges instead of replacing, the content predicate that latched the clears is deleted, and new **I-30** binds the three together in try-state. **SQ-88** ‚Äî the row claims A8 sources the previous settled Baseline TWAP "via a market seam"; the production adapter actually reads `RecentCohortSummaries` for epoch e‚àí1, which cannot settle until e+2, so **05 ¬ß5.3's carry path is unreachable in production** and the pallet test passes only because the mock injects the value. Contract half landed; the source fix is X. **Process finding worth keeping:** the doc authors were launched before the code and two of them *refused to write spec text describing unshipped types* ‚Äî correctly, since that is the exact drift this programme exists to remove. The order was flipped to code-first, and on the re-run all three authors reported **zero discrepancies** against shipped code. Merged main's **B14** (`73b9f6d`) mid-batch; it independently corroborated the SQ-108 ruling ("No 02 surface") while broadening its facts from three VIT pots to ten genesis-endowed protocol accounts, so 08 ¬ß2.1 was written against post-merge reality. Narrative in the Decision log; this paragraph is status only. **Next:** the backlog is **D** (doc-truing, 30) and **X** (code, 72) ‚Äî no ratify or contract batch remains. In X, SQ-40 and SQ-36 still lead, now joined by SQ-88.

**Milestone B14 ‚úÖ done 2026-07-21 ‚Äî the R-4 genesis endowment, and a book-account ruling that went the other way.** 03 ¬ß7 R-4 has always required the ledger sovereign, treasury sub-accounts and book accounts to be "genesis-endowed and can never be reaped". Nothing endowed anything: genesis set `accounts: vec![]`, so the last full redeemer of the last open vault hit `Token(NotExpendable)` on a redemption 03 ¬ß5.3 makes legal. Ten statically derived protocol accounts are now endowed with exactly `min_balance`, and the regression is proven in both directions rather than asserted ‚Äî restore `accounts: vec![]` and the test fails with the real error. Writing the rule around its *mechanism* (under `Preserve`, reducible balance is `balance ‚àí min_balance`, so the endowment binds only where every custody path preserves) immediately exposed a second gap the old wording hid: MAIN's one custody outflow used `Expendable`, which ignores the floor entirely, so endowing MAIN would have accomplished nothing. **The milestone's own open sub-question was the interesting part, and the convenient answer was wrong.** The row asked whether R-4 can bind *book* accounts at all, since they are created at Seed rather than genesis, and flagged that R-4 "may need truing". It does ‚Äî but not in the direction that lets the milestone off. Per-market accounts genuinely cannot be genesis-endowed, yet a **Baseline** book holds plain USDC, because with no mirror leg to merge against the book itself funds the payout and retains the sell-side fee. Below `min_balance` that retention is unpayable under the same `Preserve` rule, and a binary search against real `pallet-assets` custody put the boundary at 6,349,782 planck (fails) ‚Üí 6,349,783 (passes, retaining exactly `min_balance`): with `MinTrade` = 1 USDC, the entire band of ordinary Baseline sells up to ‚âà6.35 USDC was being rejected. It was **doubly masked** ‚Äî the market pallet's mock genesis-endows its shared BOOK account, and the in-memory differential oracle models no custody at all, so `market-core`'s own `minimum_trades_with_dust_fees_are_admissible` cheerfully asserts the trade routes while production rejects it. That is the same blindness class the reference-model differential is supposed to catch, and it did not. Fixed by endowing the Baseline book at Seed from `POL_BASELINE`, inside epoch `tick`'s storage layer so a failed endowment rolls the seed back. `Expendable` was evaluated for that path and rejected: it folds the sub-minimum dust into the debit, sweeping the book's accrued fee into the sovereign as excess escrow where L-2 (`liability ‚â§ custody`) cannot detect it ‚Äî silently wrong beats loudly wrong only for the attacker. **Two independent reviews then caught a critical defect in the fix itself, plus a false claim in my own spec text.** The Baseline endowment was first written as a hard `?`. If `POL_BASELINE` held exactly `min_balance + headroom`, the seed consumed the headroom and the `Preserve` transfer then found reducible = 0, propagating `CoreError::Ledger` out of `tick`'s storage layer and reverting **every proposal in the batch** ‚Äî permanently, since each retry fails identically. Reachable in practice precisely because the protocol's own POL floor telemetry carried no per-book allowance, so an operator funding to the reported floor lands exactly on the cliff. That is a worse failure than the one B14 set out to fix: I had traded a bounded trading limitation for a chain-wide liveness wedge. It is now best-effort and idempotent behind an affordability pre-check ‚Äî a shortfall degrades to the pre-B14 small-sell rejection and the book simply opens unendowed ‚Äî and the floor computation carries the allowance; the wedge is pinned by a regression that failed `ModuleError { index: 61, error: [15,‚Ä¶] }` before the fix and passes after. The reviews also **disproved my own R-4 text** where it claimed the reaped Baseline residue is "recoverable under R-6": `recover_foreign` refuses `Usdc` outright and moves no custody at all, so the residue is simply **unrecoverable**, and the rule now says that plainly rather than pointing at a path that does not exist. R-4's blanket "every custody path MUST preserve" was an over-claim too ‚Äî the ledger `TREASURY` sub-account is deliberately XCM-addressable and the SDK's `FungiblesAdapter` withdraws `Expendable` unconditionally ‚Äî so that seam is now named instead of wished away. Spec trued per R-1 across 03 ¬ß1 (its "1-unit genesis endowment" was a flat contradiction ‚Äî below `min_balance`, protects nothing), 03 ¬ß7 R-4, 04 ¬ß6.1 and 08 ¬ß1.4; **no 02 surface touched, so no contract bump**. Carried forward, not filed as new rows on standing instruction: `Market::reap` does not require ledger positions to be swept before it unregisters a book's protocol-account status, and the Baseline book's floor plus accrued fee stay in the account after reap, recoverable only under R-6. **Next:** the remaining ratification batches (B5/B6) and the X-row backlog, led by SQ-40, SQ-36 and SQ-309.

**Batch B6 (ratify 13/15 ‚Äî parameters and the testing regime) ‚úÖ CLOSED 2026-07-20 ‚Äî all 19 rows disposed; open 149 ‚Üí 131 on the merged base.** 18 ratified with spec amendments, 1 refused and reclassified to batch X. Taken out of order because 13/15 are the two docs the CI gates are generated from, so drift there is drift in what CI actually enforces ‚Äî and the merge proved the point: the batch's own rule-6 edit broke a generated gate (below). Merged onto post-B2/B3/B4/B5 main ‚Äî and with B6 closed, **all six ratify batches B1‚ÄìB6 are done**: the ratify-as-shipped programme is complete. B1's lesson held again and harder: **verification broke or materially corrected 9 of the 19 rows** before a word of spec text was written. Three rows carried false load-bearing facts ‚Äî **SQ-273** claimed a 3√ó exit threshold would reopen "14" griefing flips when the ratified artifact records **62** (the 14 was a superseded N=3000 mini-run, and the "thin-capture/gate-suppression" label was wrong too: the mix is displace-and-hold 29 / thin-capture 21 / belief-capture 12, with *zero* baseline-suppression); **SQ-269** silently dropped the word **decidable-harm** from its quote of the 15 ¬ß4.9 criterion, which is the difference between a passable gate and one that CODE (1.99 %) and META (2.22 %) fail outright; and **SQ-227** asked where to assign I-13's fuzz obligation when I-13 already had a ‚â•10‚Å∂-case property suite against an exact-rational oracle ‚Äî landed in S1 the same day the S2 reviewer raised the question, from a different worktree. That suite had already found and fixed a real production defect. **SQ-158 was refused ratification and split:** `grd.bond` is faithfully a kernel constant, but ratifying `dis.merit_min` as "derived at consumer" would have silently deleted an independent values-layer raise that *two* spec texts assert ‚Äî the shipped code hardcodes equality to `B_1` ‚Äî so it moves to X as code, not a ruling. One genuinely new finding rode the batch: `UndecidingTimeout` binds at **zero margin** against the 7-day entrenched track prepare period, benign today but a trap for anyone raising that period later ‚Äî now pinned normatively in 13 ¬ß3.4 **and machine-checked** by a runtime test. **The dual review then caught three things the ratification itself got wrong** (1 blocker + 2 blockers across the two reviewers, all fixed): the SQ-194 rationale sentence appended to 13 rule 6 **broke the G0 Phase-0 gate** (its extractor read trailing prose as phantom class suffixes ‚Äî fixed structurally, with a regression test), and two of my own amendments over-ratified ‚Äî `grd.bond`'s metadata claim silently covered allowances that carry no metadata surface, and the sentinel note claimed "every inflow is admissible" when the two phase-3 caps gate independent checks. The review also exposed a real cross-doc contradiction: **05 ¬ß5.2** still asserted `gate.v_min` as an identity while the key is amendable, now amended to bind the live value. Because of the gate fix and the new test the batch is **no longer docs-only**. Narrative in the Decision log; this paragraph is status only. **Next:** no ratify batch remains ‚Äî the backlog is now **D** (doc-truing, 30), **C** (the single integration-contract v6 bump, 32) and **X** (code, 69).

**Batch B5 (ratify 12 ‚Äî ops, monitoring, release evidence) ‚úÖ CLOSED 2026-07-20 ‚Äî all 16 rows disposed; open 165 ‚Üí 149 on the merged base.** The batch was worth more as an audit than as a ratification, exactly as B1 predicted: **six of the sixteen rows described the code inaccurately**, and each was ruled on what ships rather than on what the row claimed. SQ-109 credited a strict vesting lock for the no-fees-before-cliff property, but `UnvestedFundsAllowedWithdrawReasons = TRANSACTION_PAYMENT` literally *permits* fee payment ‚Äî the strictness is emergent from `FungibleAdapter` ignoring withdraw reasons, so 08 ¬ß2.1 now binds the **adapter** and an adapter swap becomes a spec violation instead of a silent regression. SQ-137 would have ratified "real `System.Events` bytes" as per-variant evidence when all 90 event entries record the *same* blob at the *same* block. SQ-124's `for: 1h` guard is neither Alertmanager's nor shipped. SQ-238's "600 blocks" is doc 12's own ¬ß1.4(6) deadline, not a 13/08 value. SQ-239's default rule is not what the checker enforces, and left RB-BOOTNODE's two-owner span undetermined. SQ-243(b) claims an alert that cannot fire ‚Äî its series is an O3 seam with no producer. **Two rows were strengthened rather than ratified**, because fail-closed there is also *silent*: SQ-242 fixes a **‚â• 2 release-key signature floor** (12 previously fixed ‚â• 2 attestations but left release signatures unquantified, and the monitor would accept an operator-configured 1), and SQ-264 turns the coretime genesis seats into a **Phase-3 entry gate** with explicit unfilled seats in the template and validator, since a genesis that forgets them is indistinguishable from one awaiting the ceremony. Three consumer gaps were closed in tooling rather than papered over: env-evidence `tier` was produced but unvalidated (a `g1` file satisfied the release gate), the monitor's signature floor, and the missing genesis seats. SQ-241 was settled **in doc 12 alone** ‚Äî 02 ¬ß12 is already complete on the writer side and only reader behaviour was open ‚Äî so `INTEGRATION_CONTRACT_VERSION` stays at **v5** and no 02 ¬ß13 change-control cycle was triggered (the new 12 ¬ß3.1 rules do bind frontend readers, and ride the user's standing dual-side authority under R-1). SQ-135 was deliberately written **source-agnostic** so it cannot pre-empt SQ-219 (batch B6). No runtime code touched. Rebased onto main after B2/B3/B4 landed concurrently; the one semantic conflict was RB-UPGRADE's escalation, where B2 had refuted "guardians own the `PB-MIGRATION` activation" ‚Äî main's correction is kept and the paging-on-arrival rule composed onto it. **Next: batch B6** (ratify 13/15, 19 rows) ‚Äî the last ratify batch, since B1‚ÄìB5 are now all closed; then D (30), C (32) and X (68).

**Batch B3 (ratify 07 ‚Äî oracle and registry) worked 2026-07-20 ‚Äî all 9 rows closed, 6 of the 9 successors fixed in the same session, backlog 178 ‚Üí 173 on top of main's B1 close, and the oracle's escalation ladder found to be unimplementable as specified.** Seven rows ratified into spec text, one split-ruled, one refused. As in B1, verification-before-amendment was what the batch was worth: it corrected or broke the premise of **five of the nine rows**. An adversarial review then caught a **blocker in the amendments themselves** ‚Äî the registry ack row had been given `WindowAcknowledged` as its event, which is frozen in 02 ¬ß7.2 for the *oracle*, is deliberately not emitted by the registry, and would have pre-empted open contract row SQ-68. Exactly the B1 failure shape, caught and fixed before close; the same review also forced 05 ¬ß4.3 to actually *own* the milestone `target` field rather than 07 declaring it remotely.

Ratified: 07 ¬ß6.1's per-game freeze of `{B_1, R_max}` (SQ-249) and the previously *unspecified* bond divisor and rounding direction (SQ-260 ‚Äî ruled **ceil**, on the custody principle I-4/I-28 make explicit: over-custody is dust, under-custody an unbacked claim); the registry's 72 h / quorum-2 kernel floors as a deliberate divergence from the META-tracking oracle (SQ-74); the watchtower ack row ¬ß7 never had (SQ-121); welfare-pulls-not-registry-pushes with a normative reap floor replacing a borrowed one (SQ-72); the milestone `target` as a frozen per-MetricSpec field (SQ-71); and **I-28** in 15 ¬ß1 for registry bond custody (SQ-73).

**Fixed in-session rather than carried as successors:** the ceil base-unit product with a pinning test (SQ-289), `reap_epoch` moved to the general tranche (SQ-297), a zero milestone target now fail-closed at *both* entry points instead of fabricating an aggregate of 0.0 (SQ-291), and negative tests pinning the registry's non-tracking of `orc.window`/`wt.quorum` (SQ-290). SQ-292 folded into SQ-175 (neither half is independently verifiable), and SQ-76 was reclassified **D ‚Üí X** ‚Äî its "or reuse `ledger.archive_delay`" option is foreclosed by the new independent-pin MUST, and the runtime's current `ArchiveDelay = LedgerArchiveDelay` binding is safe on the numbers but non-conforming.

**The two hard findings.** **SQ-293** ‚Äî oracle round bonds, reporter stakes and watchtower stakes are numeric bookkeeping: no fungible type on the `Config`, and no path escrows, forfeits or transfers value, so ¬ß5.5's 40/60 forfeiture and ¬ß3/¬ß4's "held" stakes are notional. It is masked only because SQ-174 makes `StakeAtRisk` return `Balance::MAX`, overflowing `round_bond` and refusing every exposure-bearing report. Designing the fix exposed that the ladder is **unimplementable as written**: ¬ß6.2 prices rounds 2‚Äì3 "(each side)" but no call let the *reporter* post their half, and ¬ß11(1) destroyed the bond stack that ¬ß11(2) and ¬ß11(4) both dispose of. Both are now amended ‚Äî ¬ß5.3 gains `counter_report` with non-defense resolving against the reporter, ¬ß11(1) retains the round non-money-bearing until the verdict ‚Äî and **neither needed a contract bump**, since 02 ¬ß7.2 freezes the oracle's storage and events, not its call list. The implementation is fully specified and sized as its own audit-scope-A milestone. **SQ-296** ‚Äî ruled: value-scale the filing bond and retire ¬ß7's unimplemented escalation-time re-bond, since pricing a claim when it is made beats pricing it only if someone escalates. The ¬ß12 merit-floor objection was checked and does **not** bind (ProcessHold gates on `is_oracle_round()`; registry filings cannot hold decisions at any bond size), so the bps is pure S4 calibration; implementation blocked on SQ-174.

**Next:** B2 (ratify 06/09, 15 rows); SQ-293's implementation and the SQ-92 ledger fix as audit-scope-A milestones.

**Batch B4 (ratify 04/08) worked 2026-07-20 ‚Äî 12 of 23 ratified, 3 closed, 8 refused; open 178 ‚Üí 168 on the merged base.** All 23 rows were verified against the implementation by five parallel subagents before any amendment was written, and the refusal rate is the batch's main result: **a third of B4 was not ratify-as-shipped at all**, so the triage sweep's "conservative reading already shipped, no production code" classification does not survive code verification and should not be trusted for B2/B3/B5/B6 without the same pass. Ratified into spec text: 08 ¬ß4.1 gained rounding/tolerance *and* freeze/re-derivation paragraphs (SQ-39, SQ-56) ‚Äî the floors are frozen constants that mix three rounding conventions, so the doc now fixes the safe *direction* (every class floor ‚â• the exact requirement, ¬±10 USDC conformance) rather than inventing a rule, and SQ-56 was only ratifiable **with** its 13 ¬ß5 rider, since `pol.budget_epoch` (min `Perbill(0)`, `max_delta: None`) can be lowered by one META decision and push the true floor above the frozen literal; 08 ¬ß1.1 where per-line budgets bind (SQ-52), ¬ß1.2 INSURANCE outside NAV (SQ-207's accounting half), ¬ß1.3 enforcement layering ‚Äî the aggregate ask cap is **not** orphaned, `static_check`/`queue_time_check` both bind it pre-decide (SQ-51), ¬ß1.4 `KeeperBudgetLow { remaining }` (SQ-119), ¬ß4.4 reruns not budget-charged because `delayed_once`/`rerun` are one-way flags capping exposure at 2√ó (SQ-247), ¬ß6.3 the closed decision-critical list with the ORACLE-line carve-out and effective-exhaustion-plus-latch (SQ-115, SQ-120), ¬ß7 the contest-capital truing SQ-231 left behind (SQ-185); 04 ¬ß2 reap ordering with its 03 ¬ß5.4 companion (SQ-258), ¬ß7 the clamp index `k` (SQ-41), ¬ß8.3 `BaselineMarketOf` retention (SQ-66's doc half); 05 ¬ß4.7 recording cardinality (SQ-122). Three rows closed without amendment: SQ-65 (B1b already collapsed seeding to one split per pair; the row's premise conflated the commitment meter with escrow) and SQ-85/SQ-184 (both obsoleted by contract v5, which deleted the TREASURY ask/NAV threshold outright ‚Äî 13 explicitly declines to own a key that no longer exists). **Eight rows refused ratification and moved to X, two of them serious.** **SQ-40:** the spec already rules twice that an undefined prize proxy MUST NOT pass, but the runtime turns it into `Err(BadDecisionInput)` and fails the `decide` *dispatch* instead of returning `Reject(SecuritySizing)`, so every non-TREASURY proposal is unresolvable ‚Äî lock held, POL standing against NAV, vault unresolved, bond unrefunded ‚Äî while the reference model independently **adopts** at prize 0; the differential corpus is blind to both halves because it coerces absent envelopes to 0 and re-implements the prize table inside the harness. **SQ-36:** one in-bounds META raise of `ledger.pos_dep` (0.1 ‚Üí 1.0, `max_delta: None`) drains the shared deposit pool 10√ó faster than it filled, and after the underflow every count-decreasing ledger call fails permanently ‚Äî no migration hook exists. The rest: SQ-64 and SQ-66(b) publish wrong data on frozen 02 surfaces (`MarketCreated.epoch` ‚â° 0; `BaselineMarketOf` deleted ~307 days before ring eviction), SQ-42 is a kernel‚Üîoracle precision non-equivalence, SQ-114's own premise is false (probe response delivery draws unbounded sovereign DOT outside `res.probe_amount`), SQ-207's INSURANCE balance is unreachable by any dispatchable forever, and SQ-251 is a permanent phase-gated lock on protocol trap recovery. **SQ-298** raised: `ORACLE`, `REWARDS` and `ops.collators` carry normative budgets with no consumer implementation at all. **An adversarial Codex pass over the finished rulings then caught a defect in this batch's own amendment**: the new ¬ß4.1 text called the multi-slot rows non-arming, which contradicts ¬ß2.5's own words ("the Phase-4 arming floor") ‚Äî both were reworded to separate the per-class ¬ß4.2 arming gate from the full-slate funding target. The same pass closed SQ-185's last thread (05 ¬ß5.6's `C_hold` gloss still described `V_win` as organic flow *merely capped* against wash trading, the framing SQ-231 retired) and raised three rows outside B4's scope: **SQ-299** (04 ¬ß7a describes a storage-read POL exclusion the code achieves structurally), **SQ-300** (08 ¬ß5.3's prize-scaled `pol.b`/Œ¥ are normative but the runtime seeds flat values ‚Äî precisely the configuration ¬ß5.3 says its own non-bindingness bound does not cover) and **SQ-301** (¬ß5.4(a)'s CODE example seeds the unscaled `b` its own ¬ß5.3 rule forbids).  **Rebased onto main after #117/#119 landed:** main allocated SQ-286/287/288 and then batch B3 took SQ-289‚Ä¶SQ-297 concurrently, so B4's ten new rows were renumbered to SQ-298‚Ä¶SQ-307 (main wins, per the standing collision rule). The rebase also surfaced **SQ-307** ‚Äî applying B4's freshly-ratified 04 ¬ß2 / 03 ¬ß5.4 reap-ordering rule to `d77b6f5` shows the epoch-VOID Baseline path writes its terminal marker with no latch, so a voided epoch's Baseline book can never be reaped and its POL commitment stays a live NAV obligation forever. SQ-92's fix closed a stranded-funds leak and opened a standing-obligation one; the rule B4 ratified is what makes it visible. **Next:** B3 (ratify 07, 9 rows) is unblocked and untouched; B2 is in flight; the B4-sourced X rows want an owner, led by SQ-40 and SQ-36.

**Batch B2 (ratify 06/09) ‚úÖ CLOSED 2026-07-20 ‚Äî all 15 rows disposed; open 178 ‚Üí 174.** 8 ratified as shipped, 6 refused as code defects and then **ruled** rather than left open, 1 to the contract bump, 4 raised. The batch reads as a case against rubber-stamping, twice over. Verification broke the "ratify-as-shipped" premise on **7 of 15 rows**; a second adversarial pass over those refusals then **refuted three claims the refusals themselves had made**, and all three corrections are recorded in the rows: SQ-97's recommended fix (gate the attestation branch on `grace_end`) would have introduced a *new* deviation, since 05 ¬ß1.3 T16 attaches a grace clause to `NotRatified` and none to `AttestationMissing`; SQ-132(d)'s "will kill a live, lawfully-progressing cursor" is structurally unreachable, because a cursor's existence forces `OnlyInherents`; and SQ-45(a)'s "widening" is fail-closed in production, since the trigger it reads has no writer. Ratified into spec text: **06** ¬ß3.3's node-counting rule for `MAX_NESTED` with its reference pseudocode corrected (SQ-31), ¬ß6.2's no-effect activation and activation-expiry bounds (SQ-278, SQ-45a), ¬ß6.3's renewal-from-renewal-block edge (SQ-45b), ¬ß7's held-bond challenge basis (SQ-248); **09** ¬ß1.2(11)'s decode-depth invariant plus a new 13 ¬ß2 kernel row (SQ-225), ¬ß2.1(7)'s relay-Abort arm (SQ-131), ¬ß2.3's `updated_at` semantics (SQ-133), ¬ß3.2's trigger-list amendment (SQ-132a‚Äìc), ¬ß5.2's split-path and mint-step scopes (SQ-252, SQ-253). **Ruled on re-verification, each staying open in X until its code lands:** SQ-97 ‚Äî the attestation dispatch check is over the *record*, never the roster, so a routine attestor rotation can no longer terminally kill a ratified, matured upgrade; SQ-132(d) ‚Äî "stalled" is a time budget, not a cursor-motion test; SQ-129 ‚Äî inflow caps bind before the mint; SQ-45(c) ‚Äî the review deadline is a duration, not an epoch index, so five approvers can no longer lose 25,000 VIT each up to an epoch early; SQ-127 + SQ-144 jointly ‚Äî the PB-MIGRATION audit anchor moves to code-application time and drops its incoherent state-root component. None of the five needs a contract bump. Each row carries its implementation scope and stays **open** in X: the question is answered, the code is not written, and marking them resolved would assert otherwise. **One of the four new rows is serious: SQ-309** ‚Äî with `KeepStuck`, any live migration cursor forces `ExtrinsicInclusionMode::OnlyInherents`, so 09 ¬ß3.2(3)'s designated recovery lane cannot be included in a block at all, and ¬ß3.2(1) forecloses the SDK's own `ForceUnstuck` escape; latent behind `type Migrations = ()`, it arms with the first real multi-block migration. Also SQ-308 (screening/guard `enqueue` parity), SQ-310 (T12's review window), SQ-311 (T24 unreachable at the deadline). **The R-6 review of these amendments then found 3 blockers in my own text, all fixed before the batch closed** ‚Äî and that is the third time this session adversarial review caught me. (i) The SQ-97 ruling as first written left for-cause revocation *conditional* and omitted recall, so it opened a hole the shipped roster read had closed; revocation is now a mandatory MUST that the relaxation explicitly depends on. (ii) The new try-state rule was unsatisfiable ‚Äî `PendingUpgrade ‚áí anchor` fails for the ‚â• 72 h `DescriptorLeadTime` window on every healthy chain; it is now one-way in the other direction. (iii) 15 I-19, 14 TH-28 and 11 ¬ß11.5 still asserted the roster reading, so R-1 consistency was broken until they were amended too. Nine majors went with them, including an off-by-one in RB-UPGRADE's reconstructed anchor and a `max_steps ‚â§ 900` bound that had to become strict. **A second R-6 pass on 2026-07-21 then retracted the SQ-97 relaxation before it could ship** ‚Äî it keys on a "for-cause" departure that the frozen 02 ¬ß7.5 surface cannot express, which would have made it strictly weaker than the roster read it replaces. 06 ¬ß7 and 09 ¬ß1.2(5) now forbid implementing it; the shipped check and its liveness trap stand, and the way out is **SQ-312** in batch C, escalated rather than ruled because one option needs a contract bump and the other redefines recall. **Next:** B4 (ratify 04/08, 23 rows) ‚Äî B3 merged as PR #118. In X, SQ-309 leads (the `OnlyInherents` recovery trap); in C, SQ-312 gates the SQ-97 fix.

**Batch B1 (ratify 05/03) ‚úÖ CLOSED 2026-07-20 ‚Äî all 13 rows disposed; open 190 ‚Üí 178.** 8 ratified with spec amendments, 3 refused as code defects, 2 deferred rows closed on the follow-up cleanup pass. What the batch was actually worth is the verification, not the ratification: every row was checked against the code before any amendment was written, which improved five rulings beyond what the rows claimed and **broke three outright**. Two of those three were live defects, both now fixed (PR #117): **SQ-92** left the 03 ¬ß2.3/¬ß5 Baseline VOID settlement unwired, so single-sided Baseline holders of a voided epoch could never redeem ‚Äî invisible to every solvency invariant because pair holders still exit at par via `merge_baseline`; **SQ-98** was a crank-order race in which a ledger freeze par-voided via `tick` but Reject-resolved via `decide`, deciding whether Accept-branch holders recovered ¬Ω or nothing. The third, **SQ-79**, is a genuine G-1 question and stays open in batch X. Adversarial review earned its keep twice: it caught a fourth over-ratification in the SQ-90 amendment before #114 merged, and three real defects in the #117 patch ‚Äî including a stale TLA‚Å∫ T20 binding whose obvious fix turns out to break `Cardinality(matches) = 1` and was therefore reverted and documented. **The cleanup pass then corrected an over-raising habit** (user challenge: "why did you add new SQ questions instead of just reducing"): SQ-285/286/287 were re-examined and **ratified as shipped** rather than left open, and SQ-288 was reclassified out of *Spec questions* into milestone **B14** because it is a defect against an unambiguous spec, not a question. B1's own tail (SQ-187, SQ-188) closed the same way ‚Äî verified against `pallet-market` and `epoch-core`, both under-documented rather than defective. Narrative detail for all of it is in the Decision log and Session log; this paragraph is status only. **Next: batch B2** (ratify 06/09, 15 rows). Milestone **B14** is queued in Track B.

**Spec-questions triage sweep ‚úÖ (2026-07-20, worktree `spec-question-fixes`) ‚Äî the backlog is now sorted, sized and honest.** Scope framing first: with G0 and G1 ‚úÖ, the pre-G2 work that is *not* frontend is exactly **O3 + the spec-questions backlog** ‚Äî O1 depends on F11 and O2 on FE-P7, and G2 itself depends on the F-launch set, so no other non-FE milestone stands between here and G2. One production change rode the sweep: `pallet-epoch`'s `GenesisConfig::default()` moved `index: 0 ‚Üí 1` (+ pinning test) so a chain spec omitting the `epoch` patch section can no longer seat the live clock on the epoch-0 pre-launch sentinel and inherit the genesis activation relaxation (SQ-82; 05 ¬ß4.6, I-16) ‚Äî the residual ambient-sentinel coupling in `welfare-core` is recorded, not papered over. The section now carries a **mechanically-checked resolution-batch index**: all 190 open rows assigned to exactly one of **B1‚ÄìB6** (ratify-as-shipped ‚Äî the row already holds the conservative reading, so resolution is a ruling plus a doc sentence, ~95 rows, **no production code**), **D** (doc-truing, 26), **C** (one integration-contract bump, 30) and **X** (real code, 39). Three delegated rulings logged (SQ-267 suite ownership, SQ-230 label retirement, SQ-82 genesis default) ‚Äî ratification requested. **Next:** the B1‚ÄìB6 ratification batches are the largest single unblock; then C (the contract is already at **v5**, so the next bump is **v6**); then X, led by the four release-manifest blockers (SQ-205 ‚Äî still no production caller of `set_reserve_impaired`; SQ-263; SQ-261; the SQ-173/174/175/177/180/181/182 adoption-input family) and the self-locking liveness traps (SQ-215, SQ-235), with SQ-233 promoted by G1 as the named blocker for drill 08.

**G1 ‚úÖ ‚Äî Phase-1 exit reached 2026-07-20 (worktree `milestone-G1`, branch `feat/g1-phase1-drills`). drill 04 re-gated on SQ-282. **Drill 08**: the ~3-day D-14 lead wait is eliminated (the drill reads the live `descriptorLeadTime` metadata constant), leaving only its freeze/expedited genesis **staging (SQ-233)** as the blocker. **Prior 2026-07-20 session** (SQ-274 + #105 merge): Merging main (#105) cleared G1's last upstream dependency (G0). **(1) SQ-274 resolved** ‚Äî no production `migrations.force_failure`/`retry` surface (R-7); **B5 ‚úÖ (2026-07-17).** The 15 ¬ß4.5 benchmark/weight/PoV regime is live end-to-end. **Groundwork:** the B1a-declared A8/A11 runtime wiring landed first (next paragraph) ‚Äî without it "every call and hook" was unmeasurable. **Benchmarks:** `define_benchmarks!` covers all 12 futarchy pallets (both registry instances) + the standard/Cumulus set; the `Benchmark` runtime API is real; all **121** initially-failing runtime benchmarks were repaired **benchmark-only** (bench origins, `BenchmarkHelper` seams, worst-case fixtures; zero pallets dropped; 312 benchmarks execute). **Weights:** `frame-omni-bencher` (50 steps √ó 20 repeats) generated weights for 30 pallets into `runtime/‚Ä¶/weights/`, wired into every runtime `Config` (documented exceptions: `pallet_xcm` = `TestWeightInfo` until the B4 runtime integration; tx-payment pallets have no dispatchables; `pallet_vesting` weights ride upstream `SubstrateWeight` until the next regeneration sweep). **PoV (the review's big catch):** `Preimage::PreimageFor`'s 4 MiB `MaxEncodedLen` made `decide`/`tick`/`execute` estimate ~4.2 MiB/call ‚Äî AND the fetch seam was genuinely exploitable to force a 4 MiB PoV read (fetch-then-validate). Fixed at both layers: the guard/epoch preimage seam now fetches by `(hash, recorded_len)` with the 64 KiB kernel cap enforced at record time on both paths (a mismatch rejects **without** the read; epoch `submit` gained the missing cap ‚Üí `BadProposalShape`), and `pov_mode = Measured` overrides are justified on exactly those three benchmarks (tick fixtures carry **distinct** 64 KiB payloads per item ‚Äî honest slope 68,329 B/item, tick(10) ‚âà 846 KiB); `execute` now **pre-charges** `prop.max_weight` + overhead (2.83 MiB proof ceiling) and refunds actuals via `DispatchResultWithPostInfo`. The 06 ¬ß4 **qualification-time preimage pin** shipped too (SQ-140 resolved): pin at T5, release at every pre-queue terminal, atomic T9 handoff to A11. **PoV budgets:** `pov_budgets.rs` recomputes 13 ¬ß5 items 1/2/7 from measured `MaxEncodedLen` (MarketBook **189 B**, VaultInfo **160 B**, CohortSummary **81 B** ‚Äî the three `[VERIFY]` tags resolved in 13 ¬ß5, Verification log), asserts all 94 futarchy WeightInfo fns at worst args fit the normal class (75 % √ó (2 s, 10 MiB)), and pins `decide`/`settle_cohort` proof ceilings. **Weight-regression CI (15 ¬ß4.5):** deterministic diff gate on committed weight files ‚Äî full-expression parser (split ref/proof `from_parts`, db read/write counts, component slopes), the >10 % rule applied to **worst-case totals** at component maxima, removals require acks, main-push falls back to `HEAD~1`, 16 self-tests; CI jobs `weights` + `benchmark-smoke` (pinned omni-bencher 0.15.0, wasm32v1-none). **Dual review:** spec-reviewer (0 blocker/3 major/7 minor) + adversarial Codex (4 blocker/3 major/4 minor) ‚Äî every actionable finding fixed in-session with regressions; the one non-actionable major is the pre-existing SQ-93 apply-surface conflict (now extended with the 09 ¬ß2.2 ‚Üî 11 ¬ß11.8.4/S17 FE-workflow evidence; user/joint sign-off required). **Merged onto post-B2/B3/B4/B7/B9 main in-session** (SQ-140‚Ä¶SQ-146 renumbered to clear the concurrent audits; keeper rebates composed with the pre-charge/refund execute and the pinning tick). New SQ-147‚Ä¶SQ-149; `keeper.rebate`'s fee basis (SQ-117) is now derivable from the generated weights but stays a user-gated 13 ¬ß1 edit. **Next: B6** (upgrade path e2e) is the only remaining ‚¨ú B-milestone before B8.

Combines the ledger's `fungibles`-bond custody + scoped `load‚Üícore‚Üípersist‚Üídrain` adapter with the guardian's injected cross-pallet seams: decomposed 07 ¬ß7 storage (`Filings`/`FilingCount`/`Aggregates` + internal `AckRecords`/`ClosedAt`), 7 origin-checked calls (`file`/`challenge_filing`/`ack_observed`/`crank_close`/`close_epoch`/`reap_epoch` Signed + `resolve_challenge` via `ResolutionAuthority`), real USDC bond escrow‚Üírefund/40-60-slash (split single-sourced from the core, R-7), 5 B1a-wired seams (`Watchtowers`/`Welfare`/`Epoch`/`Params`/`ResolutionAuthority`) keeping A6 independent of A5/A7/A8, `try_state` (custody-solvency + bounds), no block hooks (I-20), v2 instance-benchmarks √ó7. Core refactor (behavior-preserving, R-1/rule-4): bonds + milestone-target ‚Üí `Params`/MetricSpec seams, `kernel::ORC_WINDOW_BLOCKS` single-home, exact insurance complement. Orchestrated with **Codex subagents** (user directive): I authored the fidelity-critical code; a Codex `codex:codex-rescue` adversarial review ran alongside the native `spec-reviewer` ‚Äî both found real bugs (reap‚Üíre-close replay, milestone aggregate >1.0, a self-caught vacuous `SpecVersionMismatch`), **all fixed in-session with regression tests** and cleared by a focused re-pass; the **PR #54 Codex-bot P2** (acks on an *already-challenged* filing bypassed the `WT_QUORUM` cap ‚Üí unbounded `AckRecords`) also fixed ‚Äî 07 ¬ß4's "a challenge supersedes the quorum requirement" makes further acks moot, so `ack_observed` now rejects challenged filings. SQ renumbered 58‚Äì66 ‚Üí **68‚Äì76** on the post-#52/#53 rebase (A5 claimed 58‚Äì63, A3 64‚Äì67). **Next milestone:** A7/A8/A11 per Track-A DoD, then B1a (which owns the SafetyFilter matrix rows SQ-75 + wiring the 5 seams).

**A4 (2026-07-15, this session ‚Äî user asked for A4 next, not A2).** `pallets/origins` is now a production `#[frame_support::pallet]` **stateless shim** over `origins-core`: the 8 `#[pallet::origin]` variants (06 ¬ß3.1), the `EnsureOrigin` set (`decl_unit_ensure!` √ó8 + `EnsureFutarchyOrigin`), and `SafetyFilter` as a classifier-parameterized `Contains` (the concrete `RuntimeCall` projection is the one explicit B1a extension point ‚Üí wrapper set stays closed). All gates green. Dual review by the user's request: **spec-reviewer** cleared it (0 blockers; its single major ‚Äî `origins-core` re-declaring the `MAX_NESTED` kernel constant, the Track-M audit's routed "tunables baked into cores ‚Üí A4" ‚Äî **fixed** by single-homing to `futarchy_primitives::kernel` per 01 ¬ß5.2 / SQ-25, which also cleared the naming minor; fuzz minor‚ÜíS2, exhaustiveness test‚ÜíB1a). **A4 ‚úÖ 2026-07-15** after both reviews triaged: spec-reviewer 0 blockers; Codex's 1 "blocker" resolved to a **B1a dispatch-protocol/spec-clarity** item (the guard already uses the origin-aware path; **SQ-32** pins 06 ¬ß3.3), its majors 2/3 to documented **B1a classifier constraints**, its major 4 to **B1a/S5** runtime-level proofs + **S2** fuzz (with the one cheap nobody-under-every-wrapper test gap fixed), its minor to **SQ-31**. Two spec questions logged (SQ-31 count semantics, SQ-32 base-filter dispatch protocol) ‚Äî both spec-clarity for B1a, neither a code defect. After A4: A2, then the rest of Track A, then B1a (which must honor the SafetyFilter dispatch protocol + classifier constraints A4 documented).
**A10 ‚úÖ (2026-07-15).** `pallet-guardian` + `pallet-attestor` shipped as production `#[frame_support::pallet]`s over their frame-free cores by the same shell-over-core pattern (orchestrated with Codex + `spec-reviewer` + `test-engineer` subagents; attestor Codex-authored). Guardian: 5 origin-checked calls, 02 ¬ß6 frozen events via injected seams, bounded `on_initialize` crank with terminal reaping, I-23 try_state, a 500-step √ó 20-seed shell-vs-core differential; attestor: 4 calls, 2-of-N quorum view. Two spec-reviewer passes + a Codex adversarial pass ‚Äî every major fixed in-session with tests (guardian reaping/re-election-clear/recall-seam/I-23; attestor try_state-relax/NoOpenChallenge/single-home). Full workspace gates green (54 test suites). SQ-45/SQ-46 raised; A10 design decisions logged. **Next: A2** (`pallet-conditional-ledger`, audit-scope-A) by the same pattern ‚Äî expect the same `DecodeWithMemTracking`/mock/benchmark wiring plus the R-7 adversarial/solvency suites; then A3‚ÄìA9/A11, then B1a.

### Track A ‚Äî Protocol pallets

**Re-scoped 2026-07-14 (user-directed; see Decision log): Track A delivers production
Polkadot-SDK FRAME pallets, not frame-free models. A1‚ÄìA11 are reopened ‚¨ú.** Their logic
already exists as **frame-free functional-core models** (state struct + `fn op(&mut self)
-> Result` + typed Event/Error + `try_state`); that code is **kept as the functional
core**, relocated to `crates/<name>-core/` (`no_std`, no `frame` deps ‚Äî rule 9 / 01 ¬ß5.2),
and preserved as the differential oracle (Python M3 ‚â° Rust core ‚â° FRAME runtime) and the
WASM-portable / auditor-consumable port. A milestone is ‚úÖ only once its pallet ships in
full FRAME production style ‚Äî the **necessary parts** per pallet:

- **`crates/<name>-core/`** ‚Äî the relocated frame-free model (functional core; today's
  `pallets/<name>` logic, unchanged in behavior).
- **`pallets/<name>/src/lib.rs`** ‚Äî a `#[frame_support::pallet]` shell: a `Config` trait
  (`RuntimeEvent`, `WeightInfo`, constants via `Get<‚Ä¶>`, `Params`/currency/asset + origin
  bounds); `#[pallet::storage]` items as `BoundedVec`/`BoundedBTreeMap` (`MaxEncodedLen`,
  bounds from 13 ¬ß4, names + SCALE shapes matching 02 byte-for-byte ‚Äî rule 5);
  `#[pallet::event]` / `#[pallet::error]` mapped 1:1 from the core; `#[pallet::call]`
  extrinsics that enforce an explicit origin (`EnsureOrigin` over `pallet-origins`, one
  SafetyFilter authority-matrix row per call ‚Äî 06 ¬ß3) then load storage ‚Üí call the core ‚Üí
  persist ‚Üí deposit events, each carrying `#[pallet::weight(T::WeightInfo::‚Ä¶)]`;
  `#[pallet::hooks]` with the mandatory `try_state` (15 ¬ß1) and only the cursor-bounded
  cranks the spec allows (none where it says none ‚Äî the ledger has no hooks, 03 ¬ß10);
  `#[pallet::genesis_config]` / `genesis_build` where the pallet has genesis state.
- **`pallets/<name>/src/mock.rs`** ‚Äî a mock runtime (`construct_runtime!`: `System` + the
  pallet + its dependencies, with test `Config` impls).
- **`pallets/<name>/src/tests.rs`** ‚Äî per-extrinsic √ó error-path √ó origin-misuse tests
  through the closed wrapper set, limit/boundary coverage, and a `try_state` assertion
  (15 ¬ß4.1, ¬ß1). Audit-scope-A pallets (A1, A2, A11 ‚Äî R-7) add the adversarial /
  rounding-against-the-claimant / solvency suites.
- **`pallets/<name>/src/benchmarking.rs`** + **`weights.rs`** ‚Äî `#[benchmarks]` for every
  extrinsic and hook, a `WeightInfo` trait, a generated `weights.rs`, and the
  `runtime-benchmarks` feature. PoV-calibrated weights land with B5; the harness + stubs
  are part of the milestone.
- **`Cargo.toml`** ‚Äî `frame-support` / `frame-system` / `frame-benchmarking` on the
  `polkadot-stable2603` pins (01 ¬ß9), the `std` / `runtime-benchmarks` / `try-runtime`
  features, and the `<name>-core` dependency.

Scaffold via `/new-pallet` (already emits this shape); `test-engineer` authors the 15 ¬ß4
suites; a `spec-reviewer` pass with no blockers precedes ‚úÖ (R-6). Per-pallet FRAME wrapping
lives here; runtime-level `construct_runtime!` / `impl_runtime_apis!` assembly is B1a/B2.

ledger sweeps (positive residual, advancing partial batches, zero-residue full reap); guard `execute` (terminal dequeue only; `reap_prequeue_ratification` deliberately unrebated); oracle `crank_round_close` (terminal + latch-once extension)/`crank_reserve_probe` (**gated on `ProbeDispatch::live()`** ‚Äî the pending `()` dispatcher pays nothing)/`recompute_proof`/`ack_observed` (07 ¬ß4, OracleLine, SQ-121); | B14 | **Genesis endowment of the ledger sovereign, treasury sub-accounts and book accounts (03 ¬ß7 R-4).** R-4 states these accounts "are genesis-endowed and can never be reaped"; no genesis path endows any of them with USDC ‚Äî `runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/genesis.rs` registers the USDC/DOT assets and metadata but sets `accounts: vec![]`, and `deploy/genesis/allocations.template.json` carries none. Because `settle_collateral` uses `Preservation::Preserve`, the **last full redeemer of the last open vault** fails `Token(NotExpendable)` once the sovereign would drop below USDC `min_balance` ‚Äî a redemption legal under 03 ¬ß5.3 becomes impossible. Scope: decide which of the three account classes are genesis-seedable at all (book accounts are created dynamically at Seed, so R-4 may need truing for those), seed the ones that are, and carry the amounts through the allocations template and the chain-spec validator. | 03 ¬ß7 R-4; 03 ¬ß5.3 | B1a genesis, B3 chain-spec | ‚úÖ | **Done 2026-07-21.** Ten statically derived protocol accounts genesis-endowed with exactly `min_balance` (`USDC_CENT`) through the `ForeignAssets` genesis `accounts` field: the ledger sovereign plus its INSURANCE/BOOK/POL/POL_BASELINE/FEES/TREASURY sub-accounts, and treasury MAIN/KEEPER/ORACLE. `TreasuryPotFunding::fund` flipped `Expendable` ‚Üí `Preserve`, because an endowment is only half the mechanism ‚Äî an `Expendable` outflow ignores `min_balance` and reaps the account regardless, so MAIN's floor would not have bound. Regression proven in both directions: restoring `accounts: vec![]` makes `last_redeemer_of_last_vault_can_fully_exit` fail `Token(NotExpendable)`; with the endowment it passes with `Vaults::escrowed == Some(0)` and L-2 green. **The book-account sub-question resolved against the easy answer.** Per-market accounts genuinely cannot be genesis-endowed (their addresses embed a runtime-allocated `MarketId`), but "therefore R-4 does not bind them" is false: decision/gate books and every per-market fee account custody positions only, yet a **Baseline** book holds plain USDC because it retains the sell-side fee. A binary search against real `pallet-assets` custody put the flip at 6,349,782 planck (fail) ‚Üí 6,349,783 (pass, retaining exactly `min_balance`), so with `MinTrade` = 1 USDC the entire band of ordinary Baseline sells from 1 to ‚âà6.35 USDC was being rejected ‚Äî invisible because `pallets/market/src/mock.rs` genesis-endows its shared BOOK account and the in-memory differential oracle models no custody at all. Fixed by endowing the Baseline book at Seed from `POL_BASELINE`, inside epoch `tick`'s storage layer so a failed endowment rolls the seed back (G-1). `Expendable` was evaluated for that path and **rejected**: it folds the sub-minimum dust into the debit, silently sweeping the book's accrued fee into the sovereign as excess escrow where L-2 (`liability ‚â§ custody`) cannot detect it ‚Äî a worse outcome than a hard failure. Spec trued per R-1 (03 ¬ß7 R-4 rewritten, also repairing 03 ¬ß1's contradictory "1-unit genesis endowment"; 04 ¬ß6.1 gains the Baseline sell-side degeneration; 08 ¬ß1.4 pins funding to `MAIN ‚àí min_balance`); no 02 surface touched, so no contract bump. **Dual review (spec-reviewer + adversarial Codex) found one critical defect in the fix itself and two spec over-claims, all fixed in-session.** The Baseline endowment was a hard `?`: `POL_BASELINE` funded to exactly `min_balance + headroom` left reducible = 0 after the seed, propagating `CoreError::Ledger` out of `tick` and reverting the whole proposal batch permanently ‚Äî reachable because the POL floor telemetry carried no per-book allowance. Now best-effort + idempotent behind an affordability pre-check, with the allowance added to the floor; regression pinned (failed `ModuleError { index: 61, error: [15,‚Ä¶] }` before). Both reviewers independently disproved the amendment's claim that the reaped Baseline residue is R-6-recoverable ‚Äî `recover_foreign` refuses `Usdc` and moves no custody ‚Äî so R-4 now records it as unrecoverable; and R-4's blanket preserve-MUST is qualified for the XCM-addressable ledger `TREASURY` seam, where the SDK `FungiblesAdapter` is hardcoded `Expendable`. **A third review round (Codex on the PR) then found a P1 hole in the new chain-spec gate itself**: it matched only the USDC Location and `min_balance`, leaving `owner` and `is_sufficient` unchecked ‚Äî and `pallet-assets` genesis installs `owner` as owner *and* issuer *and* admin *and* freezer, so the release gate would have approved a production spec handing an external key mint/burn/freeze authority over all protocol collateral, with `is_sufficient = false` violating R-4's opening clause on top. The test fixture itself carried exactly that shape (owner = ALICE), which is why nothing caught it. All four tuple fields are now gated, for **both** declared assets (DOT carries the identical authority and funds coretime renewal), with five negative tests and the reported payload verified rejected. Originally raised as SQ-288 and reclassified here because the spec was unambiguous and simply unimplemented. |

## Spec questions

Open ambiguities/contradictions found in `docs/architecture/`. Record them here first;
under rule R-1 a genuine defect may be corrected in the spec directly, but log
non-obvious semantic changes and confirm with the user before diverging.
Format: `| ID | Question | Spec ref | Raised | Status |`

### Resolution batches (triage sweep, 2026-07-20)

Every **open** row is assigned to exactly one batch below, so the backlog can be
retired in coherent units instead of one row at a time. The assignment is checked
mechanically by `python3 tools/ci/check-spec-question-batches.py` (docs CI job):
ids are unique, each open id appears in exactly one batch, no batch names a
closed or non-existent id, and a batch declaring **0** rows is closed ‚Äî its
Members cell is prose explaining where the rows went, not a member list. Run it
after closing rows. Batches **B1‚ÄìB6** are
ratify-as-shipped ‚Äî the row already carries the conservative G-1/R-7-safe
behaviour and resolution is a ruling plus a doc sentence, no production code.
**D** is doc-truing (the spec contradicts itself and the code is demonstrably
right). **C** is the single integration-contract bump. **X** is real code.

| Batch | Rows | Members |
|---|---:|---|
| **B1 ¬∑ ratify 05/03 ‚Äî lifecycle, welfare, proposal machine** | 0 | **‚úÖ closed 2026-07-20.** All 13 rows disposed: 8 ratified with spec amendments, 3 refused as code defects (SQ-92 and SQ-98 fixed on `fix/sq-92-sq-98-void-baseline-freeze`; SQ-79 reclassified to X), and the 2 deferred rows (SQ-187, SQ-188) closed on the cleanup pass after verification against `pallet-market` and `epoch-core` ‚Äî both were under-documented, not defective. |
| **B2 ¬∑ ratify 06/09 ‚Äî governance, guard, upgrade path** | 0 | **‚úÖ closed 2026-07-20.** All 15 rows disposed: 8 ratified as shipped (SQ-31, SQ-131, SQ-133, SQ-225, SQ-248, SQ-252, SQ-253, SQ-278); 6 refused ratification and then **ruled on re-verification** rather than parked unanswered (SQ-45, SQ-97, SQ-127, SQ-129, SQ-132, SQ-144 ‚Äî each ruled, each still **open in X** carrying its implementation scope, since the code is owed); SQ-134 to **C** for the 02 ¬ß12 bump. The refusals were the batch's value: the ratify-as-shipped premise failed on 7 of the 15 rows. |

SQ-261, and the SQ-173/174/175/177/180/181/182 adoption-input family) and the
self-locking liveness traps (SQ-215, SQ-235, joined by SQ-251) lead; SQ-233 gained
urgency at G1 as the named blocker for drill 08. **B4 added eight rows and two of
them outrank most of that list:** SQ-40 (every non-TREASURY proposal fails the
`decide` *dispatch* instead of returning `Reject(SecuritySizing)`, so it can never
resolve ‚Äî and the reference model independently adopts at prize 0, a live oracle
divergence) and SQ-36 (one in-bounds META raise of `ledger.pos_dep` permanently
wedges every count-decreasing ledger call, stranding ~90 % of holders' escrow).
SQ-64 and SQ-66 are frozen-02 fidelity defects; SQ-42 is a kernel‚Üîoracle
non-equivalence the differential corpus is structurally blind to.

| ID | Question | Spec ref | Raised | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SQ-308 | **Screening does not mirror the guard's `enqueue` preconditions, so `epoch.decide(pid)` can revert permanently after an Adopt.** 09 ¬ß1.1 states queue-time preconditions are "enforced by the decision path **before** `enqueue` succeeds", but the guard's `enqueue` additionally requires every declared domain to satisfy `domain_allowed(class, domain)` and to exclude `InternalRootApplyUpgrade` (`pallets/execution-guard/src/lib.rs:906-912`), and `queue_time_check` (`runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/configs.rs:3236-3248`) checks neither. Concretely reachable: a CODE payload `[utility.batch_all([system.authorize_upgrade(h)])]` ‚Äî the wrapper 05 ¬ß96 explicitly blesses ‚Äî passes screening (the classifier only matches `authorize_upgrade` at top level via `is_sub_type`, so nested it projects to `InternalRootApplyUpgrade`), wins Adopt, and then makes `epoch.decide(pid)` fail with `CapabilityDenied` inside `with_storage_layer`, **reverting the whole decide every time** until the 7-day T20 stale path force-rejects with a full refund. Bond is refunded so no funds are stranded, but 13 days of market and a decided Adopt are lost. Two questions: must `queue_time_check` be a superset of the guard's `enqueue` preconditions (making `decide` total), and is a nested `system.authorize_upgrade` intended to be admissible at all ‚Äî 09 ¬ß2.1 describes a multi-item upgrade payload, which the top-level-only matcher cannot express? Guard-queue fullness (`MaxLiveProposals`) is likewise unmirrored. | 09 ¬ß1.1; 09 ¬ß2.1; 05 ¬ß1.4; 15 I-11 | 2026-07-20 (batch B2 verification) | open ‚Äî batch **X**; screening/guard parity owner |
| SQ-309 | **A stuck migration cursor makes the chain permanently inherent-only, and 09 ¬ß3.2(3)'s designated recovery lane cannot run.** `MigrationFailureToGuard::failed` returns `FailedMigrationHandling::KeepStuck` (`runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/configs.rs:588`); `pallet-migrations`' `MultiStepMigrator::ongoing()` is `Cursor::exists()`, true for `Stuck` as well as `Active`; and `frame-executive` turns that into `ExtrinsicInclusionMode::OnlyInherents`, rejecting every non-inherent extrinsic with `OnlyInherentsAllowed`. So while any cursor exists **no** extrinsic can be included in a valid block ‚Äî not the guard's signed `apply_authorized_upgrade`, not `execute(pid)` on the expedited CODE lane, not a guardian call, not sudo, and not `pallet-migrations`' own four Root-only continuation calls. 09 ¬ß3.2(3) nevertheless designates the expedited CODE lane as *the* recovery path. The obvious escape is foreclosed by 09 ¬ß3.2(1) itself: `ForceUnstuck` would expose the half-migrated layout that clause exists to prevent. Which posture does the spec intend ‚Äî (a) `ForceUnstuck` plus a SafeMode/TxPause allowlist, re-scoping ¬ß3.2(1)'s exposure rule (what the SDK's own docs recommend); (b) a recovery carried by an inherent or `on_initialize`, needing no extrinsic; or (c) accept inherent-only operation and name relay-driven `force_set_current_code` as the sole route ‚Äî the same off-chain-governance dependency this project refused for SQ-129? | 09 ¬ß3.2(1)/¬ß3.2(3); 09 ¬ß3.1 | 2026-07-20 (batch B2 closure, SQ-132(d) verification) | open ‚Äî batch **X**; the highest-severity finding of the B2 sweep. **Latent today** (`type Migrations = ()`), arms with the first real multi-block migration, so it must be answered before any MBM ships. Milestone B16 owns the stall predicate and deliberately not this posture question. |
| SQ-310 | **Does T12's rerun opening deserve its own window, or does it correctly inherit `grd.review_dl`?** 05 ¬ß2.1 T12's trigger is \"guardian review window closed\" and no such window is defined anywhere in the doc set; the runtime binds it to the same `deadline_epoch` that governs the 06 ¬ß5.4(3) slash. That coupling is what made 06 ¬ß5.4's ordering paragraph false in all three of its clauses. The SQ-45(c) ruling separates the two by one epoch and repairs **two** of the three clauses ‚Äî the rerun does then precede the deadline, and the deadline does govern slashing and recall only ‚Äî but the first clause stays false, because T12 would still fire at `action_epoch + grd.review_dl` rather than at `action_epoch + 1`. Closing that needs a purpose-built window, which is this row. A shorter purpose-built review window would return the proposer's rerun sooner without shortening guardian exposure. | 06 ¬ß5.4; 05 ¬ß2.1 T12/T13 | 2026-07-20 (batch B2 closure, SQ-45 verification) | open ‚Äî batch **X**. Low urgency and nothing blocks on it: milestone B18's fix is correct under either answer. Ride the next 05/06 touch. |
| SQ-311 | **T24 becomes unreachable at the review deadline even while the proposal is still `Suspended`.** 06 ¬ß5.4(2) bounds `uphold_veto` by the proposal still being `Suspended` (before T12/T13 opens the rerun), and 05 ¬ß2.1 names it as `VetoUpheldByReview`'s single producing site. But the failed-review path cancels **both** scheduled referenda (`pallets/guardian/src/lib.rs:1146-1153`), so once the ratification deadline passes the veto verdict is no longer reachable even while the proposal sits in the state ¬ß5.4(2) says admits it. An upheld veto is a *different verdict*, not a late ratification ‚Äî the values layer agreeing with the delay ‚Äî so cancelling it alongside the ratification referendum may be over-broad. Should the `uphold_veto` referendum survive the ratification deadline, bounded only by `Suspended` as ¬ß5.4(2) states? | 06 ¬ß5.4(2); 05 ¬ß2.1 T24 | 2026-07-20 (batch B2 closure, SQ-45 verification) | open ‚Äî batch **X**. Narrow consequence ‚Äî the delay stands either way and only the recorded reason code differs ‚Äî but 05 ¬ß2.1 calls T24's single producing site unreachable in a state the spec says admits it. |
| SQ-312 | **The attestor registry has no cause-carrying removal surface, and SQ-97's dispatch-check relaxation cannot ship without one.** 06 ¬ß7 and 09 ¬ß1.2(5) (SQ-97, 2026-07-20) move the execute-time attestation check off the live roster and onto the committed records ‚Äî which is right, and fixes a real liveness trap where one `attestor.set_members` rotation terminally rejects every ratified, matured CODE/META mandate the departing member attested. Its safety depends on a **for-cause** departure revoking that attestor's records first. Nothing on chain can express "for cause": 02 ¬ß7.5 freezes `AttestorInfo { account, bond, false_count, active }` and `set_members` takes only a member list. `false_count` is frozen and readable, but an attestor discovered unreliable after their 72 h windows closed can never be adjudicated false (`challenge_attestation` is deadline-barred), so `false_count` alone leaves the cause-less recall path open. Options: **(a)** add a cause-carrying removal call or field to 02 ¬ß7.5 ‚Äî an integration-contract change needing the v6 bump and joint backend+frontend sign-off, and it would also want the per-record revocation event; **(b)** narrow the dispatch predicate to reject only signers with `false_count ‚â• 1` ‚Äî no bump, preserves the liveness fix for routine rotations, but an un-adjudicated recall would stop invalidating outstanding attestations, which changes what a recall means. | 02 ¬ß7.5; 06 ¬ß7; 09 ¬ß1.2(5); 15 I-19 | 2026-07-21 (batch B2 closure, second R-6 pass) | open ‚Äî **ruled 2026-07-21 (batch C); reclassified to X carrying its implementation scope.** Ratified direction: **option (a)**, a cause-carrying removal surface. Option (b) ‚Äî narrowing the dispatch predicate to `false_count ‚â• 1` ‚Äî is **rejected**: it redefines what a recall means, and verification found it is weaker than the row assumed, since `set_members` rebuilds even continuing members with `false_count = 0`, so the strike state it would rely on is reset by routine rotation. Scope owed in X: a ratified for-cause removal call distinct from `set_members`, a machine-readable cause/evidence commitment, durable per-attestation revocation state, and one revocation event per affected unexecuted record ‚Äî removal and revocations atomic, covering closed-window records, preserving challengeability for still-open ones. **The 02 ¬ß7.5 surface is deliberately NOT frozen yet:** a prose-only amendment would specify behaviour the runtime cannot perform. 06 ¬ß7 already forbids the SQ-97 relaxation until it lands, and now records the chosen direction. |
| SQ-296 | **Registry‚Üíoracle escalation and its ¬ß6 value-scaled re-bond are entirely unimplemented ‚Äî and the underlying economics may not hold.** 07 ¬ß7 requires that "a registry dispute that escalates MUST carry ¬ß6 value-scaled bonds from that point, sized by the `StakeAtRisk` of the cohorts consuming `I`". No seam exists at all: `pallets/registry/Cargo.toml` carries no oracle dependency, no escalation code exists anywhere (`grep escalat` hits only oracle paths), and `required_bond()` is a flat two-way match on `reg.bond_incident`/`reg.bond_milestone` (`crates/registry-core/src/lib.rs:643-648`). Beyond the missing handoff: an **unchallenged** false S1 filing never escalates, is refunded its flat 5,000 USDC bond on `Upheld`, and drives `I` to 0 via `max(0, 1 ‚àí Œ£ severity)`, moving settlement-time `W` for every cohort consuming `I` that epoch ‚Äî structurally the "flat bonds made high-value cohorts cheap to attack" hole ¬ß6 exists to close, while ¬ß2(5)'s bond-coverage admission rule is computed in `orc.bond_bps` terms that `I`'s actual bonded objects never carry. Needs an economic ruling before an implementation. | 07 ¬ß7; ¬ß6.3; ¬ß2(5); 14; ¬ß12 | 2026-07-20 (batch B3, from SQ-70) | open ‚Äî **X, narrowed to its residual.** Ruling 2026-07-20 (batch B3): the filing bond SHOULD be value-scaled like ¬ß6's, and ¬ß7's unimplemented escalation-time re-bond is the wrong instrument ‚Äî pricing the claim when it is *made* is strictly stronger than pricing it only if someone escalates. **The ¬ß12 merit-floor objection was checked and does not bind:** the ProcessHold predicate gates on `d.is_oracle_round()` and registry filings live in a different storage map, so they are structurally incapable of holding a decision whatever their bond magnitude; ¬ß12's "the flat, smaller registry-filing bonds of ¬ß7 never do" is belt-and-braces rationale, and `dis.merit_min` has no enforcement in code at all (`crates/constitution-core/src/lib.rs:917` is a comment). The bps is therefore a pure economic calibration with no coupling constraint ‚Äî **owned by S4 and 13, not inventable here (R-2)**. **Implementation blocked on SQ-174:** value-scaling needs `StakeAtRisk` for the cohorts consuming `I`, and the registry has no such seam ‚Äî the oracle's own adapter returns `Balance::MAX` precisely because no frozen value-at-risk snapshot exists. When it lands, ¬ß12's rationale sentence must be re-trued to say the exclusion is structural. |
| SQ-295 | **07 ¬ß5.4's "the only admissible call is `oracle.adjudicate`" is false in the deployed runtime.** The `OracleResolution` origin also gates `resolve_challenge` on **both** registry instances ‚Äî `runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/configs.rs:3837` (`AdjudicationOrigin`) vs `:3941` (`ResolutionAuthority`, inside `registry_config!`, applied at `:3955`/`:3956`), both resolving to `pallet_origins::EnsureOracleResolution`. The track admits three calls with incompatible shapes (`adjudicate` carries `value`/`spec_version`; `resolve_challenge` carries only `uphold: bool`). Either enumerate the registry calls in ¬ß5.4 or route registry resolution off the track. Deliberately **not** doc-trued in B3: enumerating them would legitimize SQ-294's unconstrained call. | 07 ¬ß5.4; ¬ß7; 06 | 2026-07-20 (batch B3, from SQ-70) | open ‚Äî X / D, sequenced after SQ-294 |
| SQ-294 | **Registry terminal resolution is an unconstrained binary authority choice.** `resolve_challenge(epoch, filing_id, uphold)` gates only on `T::ResolutionAuthority` plus the filing being `Challenged`; it binds no evidence, admits no recompute proof, and decides the slash directly (`crates/registry-core/src/lib.rs:441-463`; `pallets/registry/src/lib.rs:605-614`). The oracle's counterpart *is* constrained ‚Äî grid-bounded value, `round >= round_cap`, live challenger required (`crates/oracle-core/src/lib.rs:821-854`). 07 ¬ß7 says a registry counter-round closes "by `recompute_proof` where the spec permits, else by ‚Ä¶ terminal escalation into ¬ß5 step 4"; the code does neither. Constrain the call (evidence/recompute binding) or specify the discretion normatively. | 07 ¬ß7; ¬ß5.4; ¬ß9 | 2026-07-20 (batch B3, from SQ-70) | open ‚Äî X (code) |
| SQ-293 | **Oracle bonds and stakes are numeric bookkeeping only ‚Äî no value is ever escrowed, forfeited or distributed.** Confirmed by two independent verification passes. `pallet_oracle::Config` exposes no `Currency`/fungible/hold/collateral type at all (`pallets/oracle/src/lib.rs:220`); `report` and `challenge` compute a bond, store it in `RoundState.bond`/`cumulative_*_bond` and emit an event without touching balances (`crates/oracle-core/src/lib.rs:470`, `:525`); escalation just doubles the number (`:716`); `adjudicate` records an offense and emits `Adjudicated` with no transfer (`:821`); the "slash" only emits `ReporterSlashed` (`:1164`), and watchtower ejection likewise moves nothing (`:642`); settlement drops the round and the stacks vanish with it (`:1040`). Reporter stake (¬ß3), watchtower stake (¬ß4) and the ¬ß5.5 40/60 forfeiture are therefore all notional, and a losing **challenger** has no penalty path whatsoever. The pallet admits the gap in its own header ‚Äî "Economic custody ‚Ä¶ is a B-track runtime concern" (`pallets/oracle/src/lib.rs:61`) ‚Äî but it was never wired. ¬ß13's `Œ£ held bonds == Œ£ open-round bonds √ó sides` try-state check is consequently absent (`pallets/oracle/src/lib.rs:1310`; `crates/oracle-core/src/lib.rs:968`). `pallet-registry` is the counterexample showing this is unfinished integration rather than a frame-free-core limitation: it holds real USDC via `Config::Collateral` ‚Üí `ForeignAssets`, transfers bonds into its sovereign account and pays winner + INSURANCE (`pallets/registry/src/lib.rs:193`, `:849`, `:864`) ‚Äî now I-28. **Currently masked, not mitigated:** production returns `Balance::MAX` for `StakeAtRisk` while SQ-174 is open, so `round_bond`'s checked multiplication overflows and refuses any exposure-bearing report ‚Äî the game fails closed by accident of an unrelated open row, and any round that *does* open has no custody. | 07 ¬ß13; ¬ß5.1; ¬ß5.3; ¬ß5.5; ¬ß3; ¬ß4; ¬ß6.2; ¬ß11; 15 ¬ß1 | 2026-07-20 (batch B3, from SQ-73) | open ‚Äî **X, audit scope A.** Spec blockers **ruled 2026-07-20 (batch B3)**; the implementation is now fully specified and blocked on nothing. A design pass found the escalation ladder was **unimplementable as written**, two ways, both now amended. (1) ¬ß6.2 prices rounds 2‚Äì3 "(each side)" but **no call existed for the reporter to post their half** ‚Äî ¬ß13's call list had no `counter_report`, and the core silently inflated `cumulative_reporter_bond` from a permissionless keeper crank, which under real custody would debit a third party inside a batch. ¬ß5.3 now specifies `counter_report` as a consenting signed call, makes non-defense resolve the round to the challenger, and forbids any crank from posting collateral on a party's behalf. (2) ¬ß11(1) *removed* the round at the money deadline, destroying the bond stack that ¬ß11(2)'s "verdict resolves bonds only" and ¬ß11(4)'s griefing price both dispose of ‚Äî silently refunding an attacker who rode a dispute to terminal precisely to force neutral settlement. ¬ß11(1) now retains the round **non-money-bearing** until the verdict, which ¬ß13's invariant already permitted ("in a money-bearing state"). **Neither ruling needed a contract bump** ‚Äî 02 ¬ß7.2 freezes the oracle's storage and events, not its call list, and the retention reuses existing state. Remaining: the implementation (Config collateral + an effect-channel drain mirroring `pallet-registry`, reporter/watchtower stake custody, the ¬ß13 custody try-state as I-29, mock needs balances+assets, ~3.1k test lines need funded accounts, weights + a PoV check on `crank_round_close` at batch 10). Sized as its own audit-scope-A milestone. |
| SQ-297 | **`pallet-registry::reap_epoch` is rebated from the oracle budget line; 08 ¬ß6.3 assigns reaping to the metered general tranche.** 07 ¬ß7 now specifies `General` for it (SQ-116), but `pallets/registry/src/lib.rs:665` still passes `CrankClass::OracleLine`. Change the class, update the benchmark expectation (`pallets/registry/src/benchmarking.rs:255`) and the unit expectation (`pallets/registry/src/tests.rs:663`), and narrow the over-broad "all registry cranks use ORACLE" comments at `crates/futarchy-primitives/src/lib.rs:77` and `pallets/registry/src/lib.rs:265`. `ack_observed` and `crank_close` stay on the oracle line and need no change. | 07 ¬ß7; 08 ¬ß6.3 | 2026-07-20 (batch B3, from SQ-116) | resolved 2026-07-20 (batch B3) ‚Äî fixed in the same session rather than carried. `pallets/registry/src/lib.rs:683` now passes `CrankClass::General` for `reap_epoch`; `ack_observed` (`:588`) and `crank_close` (`:605`) stay on `OracleLine` as ruled. Benchmark and unit expectations updated, and the over-broad "all registry cranks use ORACLE" comments narrowed in `crates/futarchy-primitives/src/lib.rs` and `pallets/registry/src/lib.rs`. pallet-registry 35/35 ¬∑ registry-core 11/11. **Residual (honest note):** the committed weight for `reap_epoch` was benchmarked under the `OracleLine` path; `General` additionally reads/writes the keeper meter, so the recorded weight now slightly *understates* the call. `check-weight-regression.py` passes because no weight file changed, and it cannot see this ‚Äî pick it up in the next `frame-omni-bencher` sweep (B5 regime). |
| SQ-291 | **A zero or absent milestone target normalizes to aggregate 0.0 instead of rejecting.** `crates/registry-core/src/lib.rs:729-731` returns `FixedU64(0)` when `target == 0` and closes the epoch normally; neither `Registry::file` nor `Pallet::file` checks the target. Both `runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/configs.rs:3923`'s own comment ("Zero makes filing/close reject") and SQ-175's row text assert a rejection that does not happen ‚Äî a fail-*adverse* fabricated measurement rather than the fail-closed refusal 07 ¬ß7's new *Milestone normalization* rule now requires of `register_spec`. Lands with, or before, SQ-175's MetricSpec field. | 07 ¬ß7; 05 ¬ß4.3; SQ-175 | 2026-07-20 (batch B3, from SQ-71) | resolved 2026-07-20 (batch B3) ‚Äî fixed in the same session. The Milestone instance now refuses instead of fabricating a measurement: `MilestoneTargetUnset` is returned at **both** entry points ‚Äî `file` at the door and `close_epoch` ‚Äî so no aggregate is ever derived from an undefined divisor, and state is unchanged on the failure path (G-1). Tests `a_zero_milestone_target_refuses_the_filing_at_the_door` and `a_zero_milestone_target_refuses_the_close_instead_of_recording_zero`; 07 ¬ß7's error list carries the new error. This also makes `runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/configs.rs:3923`'s long-standing "Zero makes filing/close reject" comment true for the first time. |
| SQ-290 | **Nothing pins the registry's non-tracking of `orc.window`/`wt.quorum`.** 07 ¬ß7 now ratifies that the registry holds the 72 h / quorum-2 kernel floors while the oracle tracks live META raises (SQ-74), but `tools/limit-coverage/registry.toml:428-433` binds `orc.window` solely to `Oracle::WindowClosed`, and no test asserts that amending `orc.window` leaves a registry filing's window at 43,200. A future refactor pointing the registry at live `Params` would pass every gate silently. Add a registry-side negative test and widen the registry.toml reason string to name both consumers. | 07 ¬ß7; 15 ¬ß4.6; I-22 | 2026-07-20 (batch B3, from SQ-74) | resolved 2026-07-20 (batch B3) ‚Äî fixed in the same session. `registry_windows_do_not_track_a_raised_orc_window` and `registry_quorum_does_not_track_a_raised_wt_quorum` amend the live params upward and assert the registry still pins `REG_WINDOW_BLOCKS` (43,200) and `WT_QUORUM`, so a future refactor pointing it at live `Params` now fails a test instead of passing silently. `tools/limit-coverage/registry.toml` reason strings name both consumers and record the deliberate non-tracking. Limit coverage: 178 keys, 0 unwired, 0 consumer-binding. |
| SQ-289 | **Oracle bond base-unit product still floors after the SQ-260 ruling.** 07 ¬ß6.1 now specifies `B_1 = max(orc.bond_floor, ceil(orc.bond_bps √ó StakeAtRisk / 10,000))` with the division rounding up; `crates/oracle-core/src/lib.rs:1273-1277` still truncates (`/ 10_000` on `u128`). One-line ceiling-division fix plus a pinning test. | SQ-219 | **15 ¬ß4.5 names RustSec as the supply-chain mechanism, and RustSec has a demonstrated coverage gap.** ¬ß4.5 requires "a clean `cargo-audit` (RustSec) pass"; for crates.io the GitHub Advisory Database is a strict *superset*, so an advisory can exist with no RUSTSEC id and cargo-audit is then **structurally blind** to it ‚Äî not misconfigured, blind. Linked, never instantiated.) This session implements the minimal closure ‚Äî a pinned `osv-scanner` leg gating **exactly** the complement (findings carrying no RUSTSEC id or alias), leaving everything RustSec covers to cargo-audit/`.cargo/audit.toml` so the lists never duplicate and the informational (`unmaintained`/`unsound`) classification is not lost; waivers are per-entry annotated and **expire mechanically** (a waiver matching no finding fails the gate ‚Äî the SQ-155 discipline). Ratified text is stricter than the row described: an alias confers coverage only if it is itself a RustSec record for the same package |

| SQ-185 | **‚ÄúNon-POL contest notional‚Äù lacks an operational state definition.** Specify which inventory/liquidity components count, and when, for the time-average that backs decision-grade `AttackCostHat`. | 05 ¬ß5.2/¬ß5.6; 08 ¬ß5.2 | 2026-07-17 (B1b R1 dual review) | **resolved 2026-07-20 (B4 ratification).** Stale since the SQ-231 amendment (2026-07-18): 04 ¬ß7a already gives the full operational state definition the row asks for ‚Äî which components count (`noi_t = Œ£_branch max(q_branch ‚àí q_pol_branch, 0)¬∑price_branch`, POL excluded structurally), when (backward-weighted on the observation grid, previous-block stored `q` and quote, per-event integral admissible, no credit across an unspanned boundary), the pair reduction (min, never sum) and rounding (down).
