| SQ-97 | 09 ¬ß1.2(5) states the execute-time attestation check as record "presence/unrevoked/unchallenged", but I-19 / 06 ¬ß7's bonded 2-of-3-over-‚â•3-active-members regime implies **live quorum**; a post-queue signer recall/ejection drops effective quorum without revoking the record. A11 fails closed by re-checking `has_quorum` at execute too. Confirm the intended post-queue quorum-loss semantics at a future 09/06 touch. | 09 ¬ß1.2(5); 06 ¬ß7; 15 I-19 | 2026-07-16 (A11 review) | open ‚Äî batch **X** (**RULED 2026-07-20**, batch B2; implementation owed). An adversarial re-verification **confirmed one half of the refusal and refuted the other.** *Confirmed and understated:* the unspecified active-membership predicate is not one site but **three** ‚Äî `present_unrevoked_unchallenged` requires the bound attestation's signer to still be seated (`runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/configs.rs:4784`), `has_quorum` skips any attestation whose signer is unseated (`crates/attestor-core/src/lib.rs:361`), and it re-applies the ‚â•3-active floor at dispatch (`:352`) although 06 ¬ß7 binds that floor to *qualification*. The repo's own pinning test shows the refusal misattributed the mechanism: `code_execution_losing_live_attestor_quorum_is_a_storage_noop` (`tests.rs:5697`) rotates out a member who is **not** the bound attestor, so the rejection comes entirely from `has_quorum`. *Refuted:* the missing `grace_end` guard is **not** a defect ‚Äî 05 ¬ß1.3 T16 attaches a grace clause to `NotRatified` and none to `AttestationMissing`, and 09 ¬ß1.2(7) makes meter contention the only retry-within-grace condition, so the fix the refusal recommended would have introduced a new deviation. "Routine rotation drops the ‚â•3 floor" was also wrong: `set_members` refuses fewer than three and seats every named account active. **Ruled** in 06 ¬ß7 (*What binds where*, *Ejection for cause*) and 09 ¬ß1.2(5)/¬ß2.4: live-registry state binds at qualification, the dispatch re-check is over the records. The `ChallengeStatus::Upheld` polarity collision between 09 ¬ß2.4 and 06 ¬ß7 was fixed in the same pass, and the five sites reading it backwards (05 ¬ß1.3/¬ß2.1/¬ß5.5, 15 I-19) trued with it. **The first draft of this ruling was itself a blocker and was rewritten the same day** (R-6 review): it left the for-cause revocation *conditional* ("if those attestations are to be invalidated") and never mentioned recall, so two attestors ejected for adjudicated-false attestations could still have carried an upgrade to execution on records nobody could challenge any more ‚Äî strictly weaker than the roster read it replaced, and the outcome 14 TH-28 exists to prevent. The revocation is now a **mandatory MUST covering ejection and declared-cause removal**, explicitly load-bearing for the relaxation and forbidden to ship without it, plus a rule that departure must not foreclose an already-open challenge window. 15 I-19, 14 TH-28 and 11 ¬ß11.5 row 6 were amended in the same pass, which the first draft had missed. Original refusal text: (1) `present_unrevoked_unchallenged` (`runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/configs.rs:4772-4774`) adds an **active-membership** predicate that appears in *no* spec text ‚Äî 09 ¬ß1.2(5), 09 ¬ß2.4, 05 ¬ß1.3/T16 and 15 I-19 all state the narrow revoked/challenge-upheld trigger, and 06 ¬ß7's dispatch-time re-check is defined purely over the attestations. A past, unchallenged, window-closed attestation of a specific artifact hash does not become false because its signer later left the registry. (2) The attestation branch of `queue_reject_reason` (`pallets/execution-guard/src/lib.rs:1033-1044`) carries **no `grace_end` guard** where the adjacent ratification branch does, so the failure is terminal at the next permissionless tick rather than retryable in grace. Combined, **one** attestor departure ‚Äî a routine roster rotation, or any single ejection, which also drops the ‚â•3-active floor and kills *every* queued CODE/META item ‚Äî terminally rejects a ratified, matured upgrade, with no re-bind path (`AttestationBindings` is written only at enqueue). Fix: drop the membership predicate from the presence leg or add a permissionless re-bind, and gate the branch on `grace_end`. **Implementation owed:** two halves that MUST ship together ‚Äî (1) move the roster predicates to the queue-time path, leaving the dispatch check over the records (present ‚àß unrevoked ‚àß no challenge resolved against them); (2) make a **for-cause** departure (`false_count` ejection, or a declared-cause removal) atomically revoke that attestor's outstanding attestations with a per-record event. Half (1) alone is a *regression*. Also fix that unseating an attestor currently makes their still-in-window attestations unchallengeable by anyone; add the missing positive test (a queued, ratified CODE item survives a no-cause rotation) and a membership seam in the guard mock, which stubs both predicates as booleans. **RETRACTED IN PART 2026-07-21 (second R-6 pass).** The ruling above is correct in principle and **not implementable as written**: it makes the record-only dispatch check depend on a *for-cause* departure revoking the attestor's records, and "for cause" has no on-chain carrier. 02 ¬ß7.5 freezes `AttestorInfo { account, bond, false_count, active }` with no cause field, and the only removal call replaces the whole roster with a bare member list. `false_count` cannot substitute in every case either: `challenge_attestation` is barred once the 72 h window closes (`crates/attestor-core/src/lib.rs:273`), so an attestor found unreliable *after* their windows closed can never be adjudicated false, and the values layer's only response is a cause-less removal. Shipping the relaxation on that footing would be **strictly weaker** than the live-registry check it replaces. **Therefore the shipped dispatch-time roster read stands as the status quo, and the liveness trap this row opened with stands with it** ‚Äî 06 ¬ß7 and 09 ¬ß1.2(5) now say so explicitly and forbid implementing the relaxation. Two ways out, recorded as **SQ-312** (batch C): a cause-carrying removal surface in 02 ¬ß7.5 (contract bump + joint sign-off), or a narrower dispatch predicate over the already-frozen `false_count` (no bump, but it changes what a recall means). **That choice is an integration-contract decision and is deliberately not made here.** Two premises of the first fix were also wrong and are corrected in 06 ¬ß7: challenge foreclosure after a departure is caused by the SQ-248 held-bond lookup failing for an unseated attestor, not by any membership requirement on the challenger; and "outstanding attestations" now explicitly includes closed-window records, which are the load-bearing case rather than an edge. |
| SQ-92 | VOID cohorts have no normative reap/archive policy ‚Äî retained terminal `Void` cohort records can accumulate despite I-21's bounded-collection language; and 03 (Baseline settle at `s = 0.5` for epoch VOID) vs 05 (Void cohorts skip settlement) name no owning transition. A8 keeps Void cohorts out of the non-terminal ‚â§4 bound but does not reap them. | 05 ¬ß3.3; 03 ¬ß2.3; 15 I-21 | 2026-07-16 (A8 review) | **resolved 2026-07-20 (code fix; audit scope A).** The 03 ¬ß2.3/¬ß5-mandated Baseline settlement is now wired into the epoch-VOID path. `WelfareOps` gained `settle_baseline_void(epoch)` ‚Äî welfare stays the sole settlement authority (05 ¬ß6), epoch may request a settlement but never choose its score ‚Äî and `void_cohort` calls it in the same transaction that flips the cohort to `Void`. The score is the new single-homed kernel constant `futarchy_primitives::kernel::VOID_BASELINE_SCORE` (0.5). G-1 edges: a new `LedgerSettlement::baseline_open(epoch)` precondition makes "no Baseline vault" and "already settled" silent no-ops so a VOID can never fail on this leg, while genuine ledger errors still propagate rather than being swallowed. Deliberately scoped **out** of the per-proposal `void(pid)` path: T20 vault voiding is a different VOID and the Baseline vault is keyed per epoch. Docs trued in the same change ‚Äî 03 ¬ß5 now names the owning transition and the G-1 no-op rule; 05 ¬ß7 gained item (5) distinguishing "a VOID skips welfare *scoring*" from the one settlement it still performs, resolving the 03-vs-05 contradiction the row flagged. **A third defect surfaced and was fixed with it, in `pallet-market`:** `observe_baseline_terminal` latched `SettlementObservedAt` without closing the book, unlike its proposal counterpart `observe_proposal_terminal`, while `do_try_state` requires every observed entry to carry `MarketPhase::Closed` + `ClosedAt`. It was unreachable before this fix ‚Äî on the ordinary path `close_markets` closes the Baseline book once the epoch's last proposal leaves Trading/Extended ‚Äî but an epoch VOID force-rejects through `void_cohort` without ever passing through `decide`, so nothing closed it and the new settlement became the first writer to latch on an open book. Fixed by mirroring the proposal path's conditional `close_book`. This is precisely the 03 ¬ß9 drift class (try-state violation ‚áí I-4 flag ‚áí PB-LEDGER-FREEZE eligibility under D-9), so a legal `void_cohort` would have armed the emergency brake. |
| SQ-81 | A7's `EpochNotFinalized` guard admits a snapshot/daily-gate crank only for a **strictly past** epoch (`epoch < CurrentEpoch`; 05 ¬ß4.6 winsorizes over *finalized* values). This imposes a B1a epoch-tick ordering ‚Äî the clock must advance to `e+1` **before** `Snapshot(e)` is recorded, so `Snapshot(e‚àí1)` exists when epoch-`e` normalization is frozen at epoch open (05 ¬ß4.6) ‚Äî and admits daily gate flags at **whole-epoch** granularity, not live per-day, so a mid-epoch guardian `suspend_on_gate` reaction (05 ¬ß4.7/06) would need a current-day/slot seam A7 does not expose. | 05 ¬ß4.6/¬ß4.7; 06 | 2026-07-16 (A7 PR #55 Codex-connector P1) | **resolved 2026-07-20 (triage sweep ‚Äî code-verified).** The increment-then-record obligation holds structurally and is pinned: neither pallet declares a snapshot-writing hook, the tick persists the incremented index before returning (`pallets/epoch/src/lib.rs:2088`), and welfare's clock IS that storage item (`configs.rs:3744` ‚Üí `pallets/epoch/src/lib.rs:277-281`), so `record_snapshot(e)` is admissible only after a tick moved the clock past `e` (runtime tests `tests.rs:3342-3368` and `:3161-3173`). The row's second clause ‚Äî a mid-epoch day-elapsed seam ‚Äî was explicitly conditional ("if live within-epoch daily recording is wanted") and remains unrequested; `record_daily_gate` keeps whole-epoch granularity. |
| SQ-82 | A7's genesis-activation relaxation (genesis specs activate at epoch 1, skipping the two-epoch lead) keys on `current_epoch == 0` as the pre-launch sentinel. This requires B1a to wire `CurrentEpoch` so live welfare epochs are 1-indexed (05 ¬ß4.6 "computable from epoch 1") and epoch 0 is never a live registration clock ‚Äî else a live `register_spec` at epoch 0 would inherit the genesis relaxation. | 05 ¬ß4.6; 06 ¬ß3.2 | 2026-07-16 (A7 PR #55 Codex-connector P2) | open (narrowed 2026-07-20, triage sweep) ‚Äî the **named failure mode is closed**: `pallet-epoch`'s `GenesisConfig::default()` seated the clock on the epoch-0 sentinel, so any chain spec omitting the `epoch` patch section booted there and a live `register_spec` would have inherited the genesis activation relaxation; it now defaults to `index: 1`, pinned by `default_genesis_config_seats_the_clock_on_the_first_live_epoch` (05 ¬ß4.6, "`s` is deterministically computable from epoch 1"). Shipped presets were already correct (`runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/genesis.rs:169-173`, pinned at `tests.rs:2964-2984`). **Residual**: the sentinel is still *inferred* from the ambient clock (`welfare-core` `if current_epoch == 0`), so 1-indexing remains a convention rather than an enforced invariant. Recommended closure: pass an explicit genesis flag to `register_metric_spec` rather than reading the clock ‚Äî deferred as a ~20-call-site refactor owing its own review. |

No 02 change (names unchanged; the map shape is what 02 ¬ß7's "key/value types" already implied). |

A7's `WelfareParams::validate` hard-rejects Œ∏S_lo<0.90 / Œ∏C_lo<0.85 (following 13). | 05 ¬ß4.1 vs 13 (entrenched floors, L124) | 2026-07-16 (A7 spec-reviewer) | open ‚Äî A7 follows **13** (hard floor at launch = the safe, consensus-preserving reading; 05 ¬ß4.1's "loosening" reconciles as *un-tightening down to but never below the floor*). Recommend clarifying 05 ¬ß4.1's "loosening" wording on the next welfare-doc touch so 05 and 13 agree verbatim. |
| SQ-79 | 05 ¬ß4.7 daily gate-breach flags: an epoch with **no** (or incomplete) recorded daily observations reads as `(s_breached=false, c_breached=false)` ‚Äî a deterministic default ‚Äî so gate markets settle "no breach" on a keeper/data liveness failure (arguably against G-1 status-quo). 05 ¬ß4.7 states no daily-coverage requirement (the gate *books* carry coverage‚â•95/98% at decide(), A8); the day_bitmap records breach days only, so completeness is indistinguishable from no-breach. | 05 ¬ß4.7/¬ß6; 15 (G-1) | 2026-07-16 (A7 Codex review) | open ‚Äî **RECLASSIFIED 2026-07-20 from ratification (B1) to code (X); not ratifiable as shipped.** The row asks a genuine G-1 question with economic consequences ‚Äî gate markets settling "no breach" on a keeper/data liveness failure is an *active, adopt-favourable* resolution on absent data, not a status-quo default ‚Äî so rubber-stamping current behaviour under delegated authority would be the wrong call. Code verification answered the load-bearing sub-question and makes the conservative option cheap: `SampledGateDays` already exists, is keyed **per epoch**, is written on every accepted `record_daily_gate` regardless of breach, and is readable by `gate_outcomes` (which already computes the two window epochs) **with no 02 contract change** ‚Äî 02 ¬ß7.4 names only `Snapshots`, `MetricSpecs` and `GateBreachFlags`. Honest limit: it distinguishes *zero samples* from *some samples* exactly, but no expected-day count is stored anywhere, so "incomplete" would need an additional derivation from epoch length. Recommendation: treat a gate-window epoch with **zero** recorded samples as not-decision-grade rather than not-breached, and leave partial-coverage semantics to the same milestone that defines the expected-day count. |

| SQ-70 | 07 ¬ß7 resolves a challenged filing "by `recompute_proof` where the spec permits, else terminal escalation into ¬ß5.4". A6 models the OracleResolution **verdict** via `resolve_challenge`(ResolutionAuthority) but has **no permissionless `recompute_proof`** mechanical path (07 ¬ß9 ‚Äî deterministic recomputation + evidence retrieval). Confirm the recompute path is B-track (keeper/B9), and constrain ResolutionAuthority to the verdict only. | 07 ¬ß7/¬ß9/¬ß5.4 | 2026-07-15 (A6 dual review) | resolved 2026-07-20 (batch B3) as a *question*, but **ratification refused** ‚Äî the work moves to successor rows, not a doc sentence. Every clause failed verification. (1) "No permissionless `recompute_proof`" is **false for the oracle**: it exists, is `ensure_signed`, evidence-bound (`hash_evidence(proof) == round.evidence_hash` ‚Üí `EvidenceMismatch`) and settles via `SettlePath::Recomputed` (`pallets/oracle/src/lib.rs:628-636`; `crates/oracle-core/src/lib.rs:770-802`). The real gap is the **registry**, which has no recompute path at all. (2) "`uphold` is the verdict, not a free authority choice" is **not true of the code**: registry `resolve_challenge(epoch, filing_id, uphold: bool)` checks only that the filing is `Challenged` ‚Äî no evidence binding, no recompute ‚Äî and directly decides who is slashed (`crates/registry-core/src/lib.rs:441-463`), unlike the oracle's `adjudicate`, which grid-bounds the value and requires `round >= round_cap` plus a live challenger. (3) "Deferred to B-track (keeper/B9)" is **stale**: B9 shipped with zero recompute references. Refused rather than doc-trued toward the code, on the SQ-79 test ‚Äî the shipped path is the *less* constrained one, so ratifying it would be an active choice against R-7. Split ‚Üí **SQ-294** (constrain registry terminal resolution), **SQ-295** (07 ¬ß5.4's admissible-call list undercounts), **SQ-296** (registry escalation + ¬ß6 re-bond unimplemented). |

Adding the field remains SQ-175's work. **New defect found:** a zero target yields aggregate **0.0** silently instead of rejecting (`crates/registry-core/src/lib.rs:729-731`), contradicting both `configs.rs:3923`'s own comment and SQ-175's text; 07 ¬ß7 now requires `register_spec` to refuse such a spec ‚Üí **SQ-291** (X). |

Determines whether the reap timing must key on welfare-consumption rather than a block delay. | 07 ¬ß7 vs ¬ß2 | 2026-07-15 (A6 dual review) | resolved 2026-07-20 (batch B3): **pull is normative.** 07 contradicted itself ‚Äî ¬ß7's trust boundary already said "`pallet-welfare` reads its *closed* records at snapshot time" while ¬ß7's `close_epoch` row said the aggregate is "hand[ed] to `pallet-welfare::note_external_component`". The row now says close_epoch computes and **stores** it, plus a normative *Consumption model* note: welfare pulls and the registry MUST NOT acquire a write endpoint (a push inverts the dependency and puts the relative ordering of two independently keeper-cranked calls on the settlement path). The code already implements the pull reading ‚Äî the push is a deliberate no-op (`runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/configs.rs:3892-3900`, `WelfarePullSink`), so "A6 does both" was true only of the pallet's trait surface. **Reap made normative rather than incidental:** records MUST outlive the ¬ß11 money deadline and every consuming cohort's settlement, and the archive delay MUST pin that floor independently ‚Äî today the registry borrows the *ledger's* `ledger.archive` (90 d min vs the 21 d worst case: safe, but only by reusing another component's key). **New:** the Milestone instance's aggregate is written but read by nothing in the runtime ‚Üí **SQ-292** (X). |

| SQ-74 | Is the registry's challenge window the **fixed** 43,200-block kernel floor (A6's `kernel::ORC_WINDOW_BLOCKS`, per 07 ¬ß7 "frozen constant") or does it track the META-raisable `orc.window` (07 ¬ß5 "MAY be raised via META to ‚â§ 120 h")? Divergence verified: `crates/registry-core/src/lib.rs:22` (`REG_WINDOW_BLOCKS` = `kernel::ORC_WINDOW_BLOCKS` = 43,200 = exactly 72 h at 6 s blocks) and `:33`/`:376` (`WT_QUORUM` const) vs `crates/oracle-core/src/lib.rs:492` (`params.window`) and `:694` (`params.watchtower_quorum`). Rationale ratified: registry filings hold settlement only (¬ß12) and must clear the ¬ß11 money deadline, so a longer window buys no dispute quality while pushing closure toward it. **Residual:** `tools/limit-coverage/registry.toml:428-433` binds `orc.window` to the oracle consumer only, and nothing pins the registry's *non*-tracking ‚Üí **SQ-290** (X, test coverage). |
| SQ-75 | The 7 registry calls need SafetyFilter authority-matrix rows (06 ¬ß3) + closed-wrapper negative tests (rule 6). All are `Signed` except `resolve_challenge` (ResolutionAuthority). B1a obligation, per the A1‚ÄìA11 precedent. | 06 ¬ß3; 15 ¬ß1 | 2026-07-15 (A6 dual review) | open (narrowed 2026-07-20, triage sweep) ‚Äî **half (a) is met**: both registry instances carry exhaustive no-`_` authority-matrix rows (`runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/classifier.rs:642-660`), pinned bidirectionally in the S5 inventory (`tests_s5.rs:226-227`) so a new or stale call fails CI. **Residual = half (b)**: the generated wrapper-negative suites filter to unconditionally-denied and privileged rows, so no generated composition ever wraps a registry `Leaf(Public)` call ‚Äî specifically, no test composes `resolve_challenge` under an origin-elevating wrapper and asserts denial. | SQ-76 | A6's reap archive-delay gate needs a tunable, but 13 has only `ledger.archive_delay` (no `reg.archive_delay`); the registry's `Config::ArchiveDelay` is a `Get` the runtime sources at B1a. Add a `reg.archive_delay` key to 13 ¬ß1 (+ 07 ¬ß14), or reuse `ledger.archive_delay`? Also: 07 ¬ß7 anchors reap at "cohort settlement + archive delay" while the code anchors at close-block + delay (ledger `sweep_dust` precedent) ‚Äî coincide iff the delay ‚â• the close‚Üílast-consuming-cohort settlement span. | 13 ¬ß1; 07 ¬ß7/¬ß14 | 2026-07-15 (A6 re-pass) | open ‚Äî **narrowed and reclassified D ‚Üí X by batch B3.** *Half 2 resolved:* 07 ¬ß7's storage table said "reaped at cohort settlement + archive delay" while the code anchors at close-block + delay; B3 re-worded the cell to match the code and added the normative *Consumption model* note. *Half 1 narrowed, and the "or reuse `ledger.archive_delay`" option is now foreclosed:* that note requires the archive delay to carry a floor no shorter than the ¬ß11 worst case (d21) **and to pin it independently**, so deriving it from another component's key does not conform. Today `runtime/bleavit-runtime/src/configs.rs:3946` binds `type ArchiveDelay = LedgerArchiveDelay` ‚Üí `ledger.archive` (floor 90 d) ‚Äî safe on the numbers (90 d ‚â´ 21 d) but non-conforming, and retuning the ledger would silently erode the registry's guarantee. Residual: add `reg.archive_delay` to 13 ¬ß1 (+ 07 ¬ß14) with a ‚â• d21 floor and rebind `Config::ArchiveDelay`. It sat in **D** on the premise "spec self-contradicts, code is demonstrably right"; half that premise is now the spec's and the code is the non-conforming side, so it belongs in **X**. |

The code fix is mechanical and SCALE-compatible (thread `proposal.epoch` at `configs.rs:2091`/`:2194`); severity is frontend fidelity, not function, since `ProposalSummaryView.epoch` is a second route. |
| SQ-65 | POL seeding split count: 04 ¬ß10 says "one split funds both branches' books (the mirror branch's seed is the free image of the live one)", but `seed_book` does one `do_split` **per book** (2¬∑`b¬∑ln2` escrow for a decision pair; the mirror branch-USDC is left with the treasury). The spec-reviewer confirmed the per-book reading MATCHES 13 ¬ß5.5's commitment arithmetic (`13,863 = 2¬∑10,000¬∑ln2` for the PARAM pair), so the code is consistent with 13 while reading against 04 ¬ß10's "one split". Is decision-pair escrow `b¬∑ln2` (one split) or `2¬∑b¬∑ln2` (per-book, treasury reclaims the stray pair)? | 04 ¬ß10; 13 ¬ß5.5 | 2026-07-15 (A3 Codex + spec-reviewer) | **closed 2026-07-20 (B4 ratification) ‚Äî already resolved by B1b (`73d9d2c`).** Production does **one** split per branch pair, not one per book: `market_core::seed_branch_pair` performs a single `do_split` and transfers both branch legs to their books (`crates/market-core/src/lib.rs:1154-1215`), wired for the decision pair, both gate pairs and rerun top-ups; per-book `seed` survives only for the unbranched Baseline. 04 ¬ß10's ‚Äúone split funds both branches' books‚Äù governs and is implemented. The row's premise was arithmetically wrong: 13 ¬ß5.5's 13,863 is the per-book **commitment meter** (08 ¬ß3 charges both branches by design), not a pair escrow figure ‚Äî commitment and escrow are different quantities. |
| SQ-66 | `pallet-market` reap: 04 ¬ß2 anchors reap on "settlement + `ledger.archive_delay`" and 04 ¬ß8.3 says `BaselineMarketOf` is "reaped with the market", but 02 ¬ß7.4 (frozen) says the mapping is "retained for all epochs in the `RecentCohortSummaries` ring plus live epochs; pruned in lockstep with ring eviction (‚â§ 36 entries)". | SQ-63 | On the money deadline, `force_neutralize_expired(m)` neutral-settles only *live rounds* for `m` ‚Äî but an **admitted component that received no report** has no round, so it got no `ComponentValues` entry, contradicting 07 ¬ß11(1)'s guarantee that the oracle "guarantees the entry exists by the deadline" (welfare would read an absent component and stall, or settle a cohort without the required neutral value). | SQ-59 | The `Rounds` bound is stated three ways ‚Äî 07 ¬ß13 "‚â§ 16 √ó 4 = 64", 02 ¬ß3 `open_oracle_rounds ‚Üí BoundedVec<_, ConstU32<192>>`, 13 ¬ß4 "16 √ó 4 √ó 3". | SQ-58 | 02 ¬ß7.2's frozen key + **value shapes** for `Rounds`/`ComponentValues`/`ReserveHealth` are narrower than the merged core: the game runs per `(component, epoch, frozen version)` (triple key, not the 02 pair ‚Äî extends SQ-2), and `RoundState`/`ReserveHealth` carry extra fields (per-round ack keying, bond-schedule freezing at snapshot, ¬ß5.5 slashing, probe timing) the frozen views omit. Reconciling (amend 02 ¬ß7.2 + bump `INTEGRATION_CONTRACT_VERSION` + joint backend+frontend sign-off, or split internal fields into non-FE-read storage) is a contract change the code cannot ship unilaterally under R-1 / rule 5. | 02 ¬ß7.2; 07 ¬ß13; rule 5 | 2026-07-15 (A5 Codex F21 / spec-reviewer SQ-1) | **resolved 2026-07-15 (user-delegated, R-1 ‚Äî 02 amended, contract v2‚Üív3).** The "defer to B2" reading was unworkable: A5 ‚Üí B2 ‚Üí B1a ‚Üí A1‚ÄìA11 is circular, so A5 can never reach ‚úÖ if it waits on B2. Since there is no deployed runtime (pre-genesis) and no FE yet, the contract was made **correct now** and B2/Track-F conform to it. Amended **02 ¬ß7.2** `Rounds`/`ComponentValues` to the triple key `(MetricId, EpochId, MetricSpecVersion)` + the full `RoundState`/`ReserveHealth` field sets, **02 ¬ß4** `OracleRoundView` gains `spec_version`, **02 ¬ß13** version-history entry, **07 ¬ß13** synced, **00 D-2** records the bump, and `INTEGRATION_CONTRACT_VERSION` 2‚Üí3 in `futarchy-primitives` (+ the constitution/primitives version tests). The v2 pair key was self-contradictory (its own bound note said per-version games "append a `RoundState` per frozen version" ‚Äî impossible for a one-value-per-key map), so this is an R-1 correction, not a redesign. Joint backend+frontend sign-off: the user (both sides). A drift-lock test (`round_state_fields_match_contract_02_section_7_2`) pins the shape so it can't re-diverge. |

The hazard the row half-saw is real and inverted: `pol.budget_epoch` has min `Perbill(0)` and `max_delta: None`, and `pol.b.*` max `u128::MAX`, so one META decision can lower the budget or raise `b` and push the true floor **above** the frozen literal ‚Äî the unsafe direction. 08 ¬ß4.1 gained a *Freeze and re-derivation* paragraph and 13 ¬ß5 item 6 gained the five POL keys as `RederiveBudgets` triggers. A9 adds `prune_coretime_quote` (B4-driven) and rejects zero-price quotes, but the validity-window semantics (when does an open quote expire?) are 09 ¬ß4/¬ß6 / B4 XCM territory. | 09 ¬ß4/¬ß6 | 2026-07-15 (A9 Codex review) | **resolved 2026-07-20 (triage sweep ‚Äî code-verified).** B12 supplied the missing validity semantics: inclusive-TTL freshness via `ops.ct_quote_ttl`, in-place supersession, and expiry-only permissionless `prune_coretime_quote` ‚Äî so an open quote lapses on its own and the bounded slot set cannot clog. | SQ-52 | Are per-line per-epoch budgets (`keeper.budget_epoch`, `pol.budget_epoch`, `ops.*`) enforced at `fund_budget_line`, at `spend`, or by each line's consuming mechanism (keeper rebates B9, POL seeding A3/A8)? The design is safe ‚Äî every debit is bounded by the line balance and the ¬ß1.3 rolling meters, so no unbacked claim is reachable. Surfaced while verifying: `ORACLE`, `REWARDS` and `ops.collators` have concrete normative budgets with **no consumer implementation at all** (proposer rewards and `collator.comp_epoch` are unpaid) and no owning milestone ‚Äî logged as SQ-298. |

| SQ-45 | Guardian playbook/timing semantics the frame-free core can't fully model. (a) **Per-playbook expiry:** 06 ¬ß6.2 gives each playbook a distinct expiry (PB-DEPEG ‚â§ 1 epoch, PB-MIGRATION "until migration resolves", PB-ORACLE-VOID one-shot, others ‚â§ 14 d), but the core applies one uniform 14-day (`HOLD_MAX_BLOCKS`) cap + pure time-expiry ‚Äî a *safe* under-approximation (never longer than spec) but not exact; exact bounds need `epoch.length`/trigger-flag context only the pallet can read. Must the guardian *pallet* enforce per-playbook expiry, or is the uniform cap acceptable for v1? (b) **PB-LEDGER-FREEZE renewal** (06 ¬ß6.3): is "14 further days" measured from the renewal block (as implemented: `expiry = now + 14 d`) or from the original expiry, and where does "auto-lift early when the I-4 drift flag clears" live ‚Äî guardian pallet (not modeled; expiry is time-only) or the ledger pallet? (c) **Review-deadline boundary** (06 ¬ß5.4): "within 2 epochs" is implemented as slash-when `epoch > dispatch_epoch + 2` (inclusive through the end of epoch e+2) ‚Äî confirm the intended inclusive/exclusive edge. | 06 ¬ß5.4/¬ß6.2/¬ß6.3 | 2026-07-15 (A10 guardian spec-reviewer) | open ‚Äî batch **X** (**RULED 2026-07-20**, batch B2: (a)/(b) ratified, (c) ruled; implementation owed). Verification **corrected the (a) residual's harm framing and sharpened (c)'s.** *(a) residual:* the `void_in_flight` derivation does violate the newly-ratified rule (ii) as written, but the row called it a live *widening* and it is not ‚Äî `PlaybookId::OracleVoid` can only activate on the `oracle_deadlock` trigger, which has **no production writer** (`configs.rs` leaves it in `..TriggerState::none()`, asserted by a test named `‚Ä¶oracle_void_trigger_stays_fail_closed`), so `void_in_flight` is permanently **false** in production. It is a fail-closed under-approximation that becomes a widening only when SQ-233 feeds the trigger: latent, not live. Verification also killed the obvious fix ‚Äî no on-chain "VOID in flight" signal exists to re-point at (`void_cohort` is atomic and leaves only a 32-epoch archive bit), so the fix is *add the signal*, and the interim is an explicit `false`. *(c):* confirmed as a code fact, but the row's "inclusive through the end of epoch e+2" imported an epoch-index model the spec never uses. 06 ¬ß5.4 states a **duration** ("within 2 epochs **of the action**") and its own ordering paragraph budgets ~42 days; the shipped `>=` on an epoch index yields a window of (21 d, 42 d], so a late-epoch action can lose a full epoch of review. **Ruled** in 06 ¬ß5.4: the deadline is the duration, block-denominated is exact, and until that ships the index comparison must be strict. Original narrowing: **(a) ratified** into 06 ¬ß6.2: one uniform 14-day kernel window with each row's Expiry column binding as an additional never-weaker bound, `PB-DEPEG` bound by the kernel epoch floor, and ‚Äî closing a real gap ‚Äî an activation record MUST NOT serve as another playbook's trigger source. That last rule is *not* as-shipped: `void_in_flight` is derived from `playbook_active(OracleVoid)` (`configs.rs:4217`), so a one-shot PB-ORACLE-VOID keeps PB-HALT-INTAKE activatable for up to 14 days after its condition ended ‚Äî a widening past ¬ß5.2's verified-trigger discipline, now normatively closed and owed a code fix. **(b) renewal edge ratified** (fresh window from the renewal block, total ‚â§ 28 d, 06 ¬ß6.3); its **auto-lift arm was refused** ‚Äî the early lift exists in neither the guardian nor the ledger pallet, so it is an unimplemented normative requirement, folded into SQ-233 rather than ratified. **(c) refused:** the deadline is `epoch >= dispatch_epoch + grd.review_dl` (`crates/guardian-core/src/lib.rs:560`, test-pinned `pallets/guardian/src/tests.rs:511`), so the review gets epochs e and e+1 only and 5 approvers lose 25,000 VIT each a full epoch earlier than the row claimed ‚Äî an active penalty, not a status-quo default, failing the G-1 test set by SQ-79/SQ-285. **Residual: (c) plus the two code fixes above.** **Implementation owed:** block-denominate the deadline (`action_block + grd.review_dl √ó epoch.length`; exact, but changes `ReviewRecord`'s SCALE shape) or take the strict-comparison interim (`epoch > deadline_epoch` at `crates/guardian-core/src/lib.rs:536` and `:560`, `pallets/guardian/src/lib.rs:1504`), landing in `guardian-core`, which the shell-vs-core differential suite drives from both sides. **The `on_initialize` benchmark primes the crank at exactly `deadline`, so leaving it there under a strict comparison silently measures a no-op crank ‚Äî regenerate weights.** Plus set `void_in_flight: false` explicitly and fold the real signal into SQ-233. |
| SQ-46 | The attestor challenge-resolution surface is unenumerated. 06 ¬ß7 says a challenge resolves "by a `ratify`-track adjudication referendum," but ¬ß2.1 (ratify-track scope), ¬ß2.2 (ratify `Contains` filter) and ¬ß3.2 (authority matrix) enumerate only `execution_guard.ratify` + `guardian.ratify_action` ‚Äî no attestor resolution call. `pallet-attestor` (A10) implements `attestor.resolve_challenge(attestation_id, upheld)` behind an injected `RatifyOrigin` seam; its name, origin and ¬ß3.2 matrix row are unspecified. Separately, 06 ¬ß8's event shorthand `AttestationSubmitted/Challenged/ChallengeResolved` does not distribute a shared prefix cleanly, and the pallet additionally emits `MembersSet`/`AttestorEjected` (not in the ¬ß8 list). Both need pinning when the attestor surface is frozen at B2 (rides with SQ-2/SQ-26's 02 ¬ß7 attestor-row amendment, one contract bump). | 06 ¬ß2.1/¬ß2.2/¬ß3.2/¬ß7/¬ß8; 15 I-19 | 2026-07-15 (A10 attestor spec-reviewer) | resolved 2026-07-21 (batch C, contract v6) ‚Äî 06 ¬ß2.1/¬ß2.2/¬ß3.2 now admit `attestor.resolve_challenge(attestation_id, attestation_upheld)` on the ratify track (the shipped call already enforces `RatifyOrigin`, wired to the Ratify track in the runtime), 06 ¬ß8 carries the five exact shipped names, and 02 ¬ß6 gains a `pallet-attestor` row freezing them. **The row's `Challenged` shorthand was wrong** ‚Äî the shipped name is `AttestationChallenged`. No production code needed. |
| SQ-36 | `ledger.position_deposit` is a live META tunable (13 ¬ß1, default/K-floor 0.1, max 1 USDC), but 03 ¬ß4/¬ß9 assume one deposit value and the ledger has no hook (03 ¬ß10) to rebase already-held deposits. A governance raise makes the L-6 equality `DepositsHeld == position_deposit √ó entries` fire for entries created at the old value, and refunds at the new unit would over-draw the sovereign (L-2). Is `position_deposit` frozen-in-practice, or how does a change reconcile existing deposits (a migration)? | 03 ¬ß4/¬ß9/¬ß10; 13 ¬ß1 | 2026-07-15 (A2 re-review) | open ‚Äî batch **X** (**reclassified 2026-07-20**, B4 ratification ‚Äî refused; implementation owed). Not frozen-in-practice. `settle_deposits`/`reap_one` read the **live** `ledger.pos_dep` for both the USDC movement and the `DepositsHeld` decrement (`pallets/conditional-ledger/src/lib.rs:1496-1540`, `:1715-1752`), so L-2 holds as an identity and the suspected over-draw does not occur. The real failure is a **permanent fund lock**: one in-bounds META raise 0.1 ‚Üí 1.0 (`max_delta: None`, `crates/constitution-core/src/lib.rs:1874-1883`) drains the shared pool 10√ó faster than it filled, and once `DepositsHeld.checked_sub` underflows every count-decreasing call ‚Äî `merge`, redeem, transfer-out, `sweep_dust` ‚Äî fails permanently. No migration hook exists (03 ¬ß10 ‚ÄúHooks: none‚Äù). Ratifying ‚Äútreat it as frozen‚Äù leaves the bricking call reachable; actually freezing it means `max == min` in the registry ‚Äî production code. |

| SQ-42 | 05 ¬ß4.4 C_daily renormalized weights: must the renormalized weight `w_j / Œ£_onchain w` be snapped to the FixedU64 1e9 grid **before** the `w¬∑log2` product, or is only the product floored at 64.64 (the reference model keeps the renormalized weight full-precision, flooring only the product)? Affects bit-identical daily-flag reproducibility at a threshold. | 05 ¬ß4.4 | 2026-07-15 (M3 finish spec-reviewer) | open ‚Äî batch **X** (**reclassified 2026-07-20**, B4 ratification ‚Äî refused; implementation owed). The row asks the wrong question: neither side snaps to the 1e9 grid, but they hold the quotient at **different precisions** ‚Äî Rust renormalizes at 64.64 by truncating integer division (`crates/welfare-core/src/lib.rs:868-898`), the model divides at ~100-digit working precision (`welfare.py:76-80`). 05 ¬ß319 requires bit-identical pillar values, so this is a kernel‚Üîoracle non-equivalence, not a spec ambiguity. The differential gate is structurally blind: the only `c_daily` fixture uses 0.50/0.30 ‚Üí 0.625/0.375, binary-exact on every ordering, and no fixture uses a non-terminating ratio. `C_daily` feeds the `theta_c_lo` comparison, so 1 ulp flips a gate flag at a threshold. Fix = pin one side to the other plus a non-terminating-ratio boundary vector. |
| SQ-41 | 04 ¬ß7 clamp-widening index `k`: is "k missed intervals" in `(1¬±Œ∫)^k` the **total** number of 10-block intervals elapsed since the previous recorded observation (model: `elapsed // obs_interval`, so a 60-block gap ‚Üí k=6), or elapsed-minus-the-on-time-interval (k=5)? Backward weighting and staleness are unaffected; only the clamp width differs. | 04 ¬ß7 | 2026-07-15 (M3 finish spec-reviewer) | **resolved 2026-07-20 (B4 ratification).** 04 ¬ß7 now pins `k = max(1, ‚åäŒîblocks / mkt.obs_interval‚åã)` ‚Äî the *total* elapsed whole intervals including the one ending at the record block, so a 60-block gap gives k = 6, not 5. Rust (`crates/market-core/src/lib.rs:1551`) and the reference model (`twap.py:43`) already agree and are test-pinned at `^10`; the doc was the only ambiguous party. The Rust-only inward rounding of the widened bounds was folded in as a normative I-13 requirement. |
| SQ-40 | `sec.prize.{param,code,meta}` are `[VERIFY]`/sim-gated (13 ¬ß1 row 84). While undefined, how must a conforming implementation treat a PARAM/CODE/META proposal whose capability-envelope prize proxy is 0/unset ‚Äî hard-reject under 05 ¬ß5.6's "undefined proxy ‚áí MUST NOT pass sizing", or pass with `prize=0`? The reference model currently passes (the `adopt` vector), contradicting the conservative-default reading; the reject-on-undefined behavior is modelable without inventing the `[VERIFY]` numbers. *(S4 2026-07-18: the simulation carries an explicit undefined-proxy ‚áí never-adopts negative control; the reference-model/pallet representation question stands.)* | 05 ¬ß5.6; 13 ¬ß1 row 84 | 2026-07-15 (M3 finish spec-reviewer) | open ‚Äî batch **X** (**reclassified 2026-07-20**, B4 ratification ‚Äî refused; implementation owed). Not an open question ‚Äî 05 ¬ß5.6 and 13 ¬ß1 row 86 **both** already rule that an undefined proxy MUST NOT pass. It is a two-sided conformance gap. (a) The reference model has no `undefined` representation (`treasury.py:117-141` defaults `envelope=0`) and **adopts** a PARAM proposal at prize 0. (b) The runtime returns `None` for every non-TREASURY class (`configs.rs:3266-3272`) and `epoch-core:1833` turns it into `Err(BadDecisionInput)`, failing the `decide` **dispatch** rather than returning the spec's own `Reject(SecuritySizing)` ‚Äî so the proposal never resolves: resource lock held, POL commitment standing against NAV, vault unresolved, bond unrefunded. The shipped rationale (`configs.rs:1810-1819`) defends `Err` against `NotDecisionGrade`'s 10 % slash, but T10 refunds in full on `Reject(SecuritySizing)` too. The corpus cannot catch either half ‚Äî `decision_vectors.rs:265-268` coerces absent envelopes to 0 and `:283-288` re-implements the prize table in the harness, so `in_cap_prize` is never differentially covered. |

No doc states the ~10-USDC tolerance the reference model relies on (its `nav_floor` case-fits per-class rounding behind a SPEC-NOTE). The floors are **frozen constants** returned verbatim (`crates/futarchy-treasury-core/src/lib.rs:1050-1058`), not re-derived; they do mix conventions (TREASURY/META from the whole-USDC commitment, CODE from the exact one, PARAM from neither ‚Äî ~7.8 USDC above its own exact quotient), so the doc now fixes the **direction** instead of inventing a rounding rule: every class floor sits at or above the exact requirement, so the gate is never more permissive than the arithmetic. Conformance accepts ¬±10 USDC (the shipped `NAV_FLOOR_DISPLAY_TOLERANCE`); the two multi-slot rows are named as ¬ß2.5 illustrations, not arming gates. |
| SQ-32 | 06 ¬ß3.3's two-check model ‚Äî "(b) `BaseCallFilter` **denies governance-privileged calls unless dispatched with their matching custom origin** ‚Äî enforced again by each pallet's `EnsureOrigin`" ‚Äî cannot be realized inside a `Contains<RuntimeCall>`, which is origin-blind. The implemented split (A4): the **origin-less** base filter denies the nobody row, the dangerous wrappers, and bare governance leaves (a signed submitter carries no matching custom origin); the **origin-aware** re-check (`SafetyFilter::validate(Some(origin), ‚Ä¶)`, already applied by `execution-guard-core` at execute, I-11) plus each pallet's `EnsureOrigin` runs at the governance dispatch boundary (guard; scheduler per ¬ß3.4), which then dispatches via `dispatch_bypass_filter`. This is safe/conservative and matches the guard core, but 06 ¬ß3.3's `impl Contains` reference block (`_ => static_domain_allowed(c)`) reads ambiguously ‚Äî the A4 Codex adversarial review reads it as *allowing* bare governance leaves at the base filter (origin enforced solely by `EnsureOrigin`), and warns that na√Øvely setting `BaseCallFilter = SafetyFilter` on the enactment path would `BadOrigin`-reject every custom-origin call and stall governance. Should 06 ¬ß3.3 be clarified to state explicitly that (i) the origin-less `BaseCallFilter` denies bare governance-privileged leaves and (ii) governance/scheduler enactment bypasses it and re-validates origin-aware ‚Äî pinning the B1a wiring? Code is internally consistent under the conservative reading; this is a spec-clarity + B1a-wiring item, not a current safety defect. | 06 ¬ß3.3(b)/¬ß3.4; 09 ¬ß1.2 (guard) | 2026-07-15 (A4 Codex review) | open |
| SQ-31 | 06 ¬ß3.3's `MAX_NESTED` "‚â§ 16 calls total" bound is ambiguous in scope. The prose + 13 ¬ß2 read as a **global** "‚â§ 16 calls total" ("matching the payload bounds" = `prop.max_calls` = 16), but the ¬ß3.3 reference pseudocode checks `calls.len() <= MAX_NESTED_TOTAL` **per `batch`** (direct children of one batch; no count applied to non-batch wrappers). `origins-core`'s `SafetyFilter` implements a single **global** budget in which *every* node ‚Äî each wrapper **and** each leaf ‚Äî consumes one, so a flat `batch` of 16 public leaves (17 nodes) is rejected `TooManyCalls` even though its 16 leaf-calls equal `prop.max_calls`. This decides whether a maximum-size committed payload can be enacted as one batch through the execution guard's execute-time `SafetyFilter` re-check (I-11; A11/B1a). The core's global-node reading is the conservative, G-1-safe one but may over-reject a legitimate maximal batch. Which is normative ‚Äî global-node count, global-leaf count, or per-batch direct-children? | 06 ¬ß3.3; 13 ¬ß2 (`MAX_NESTED`) | 2026-07-15 (A4 spec-reviewer) | **resolved 2026-07-20 (batch B2 ¬∑ ratified).** The global-node reading is normative: 06 ¬ß3.3 gained the counting rule ‚Äî every node, wrapper and leaf alike, costs one unit of a **single budget shared by the whole evaluation**, never reset per wrapper ‚Äî and its reference pseudocode was corrected from the per-`batch` form that created the ambiguity (that form bounds no total, so it cannot deliver ¬ß3.3's own bounded-weight guarantee); 13 ¬ß2's `MAX_NESTED` row carries the note. **The row's stated rationale was refused, not ratified:** the guard payload is a top-level `BoundedVec` of `RuntimeCall` bounded by `prop.max_calls` (09 ¬ß1.2(11)), *not* a `batch`, so a maximal 16-call payload costs exactly 16 units and executes ‚Äî verified symmetric at screening (`classifier.rs:103`) and execute (`execution-guard/src/lib.rs:1233`). There is no liveness trap. The only observable cost is user-facing: a directly submitted `utility.batch` admits 15 children, not 16. |

| SQ-3 | `AttestationMissing` has two mandated producing sites across the doc set, which 05's exclusivity clause forbids. 05 ¬ß1.3 declares "every variant has exactly one producing site ‚Ä¶ MUST NOT emit from any other site" and maps `AttestationMissing` to `decide()` step 10 ‚Üí T10; 05 ¬ß2.1 T16 enumerates the `Queued ‚Üí Rejected` reason set as `{ StaleQueue, NotRatified }` only. But 09 ¬ß1.2(5) and 15 I-19 require the execution guard's **dispatch-time** re-check to produce `Rejected(AttestationMissing)` (post-queue attestation revocation), which is a `Queued ‚Üí Rejected(AttestationMissing)` transition. The A11 (#40) fix implements the 09/I-19 behavior (guard propagates `AttestationMissing` through `epoch.expire_or_stale_queue` ‚Üí T16-shaped reject ‚Üí T21), so it is compliant with the execution guard's owning doc but violates 05's producer-map exclusivity and T16's closed reason set. **Recommended resolution:** extend 05 ¬ß2.1 T16's reason set to include `AttestationMissing` and relax 05 ¬ß1.3's "exactly one producing site" clause for this variant (option a) ‚Äî dropping the dispatch-time producer (option b) would remove a security check (I-19). Needs a spec decision, not a code change; the code already tracks 09. | 05 ¬ß1.3, ¬ß2.1 T16 vs 09 ¬ß1.2(5); 15 I-19 | 2026-07-14 | **resolved 2026-07-14** (option a, user-approved; 05 ¬ß1.3/¬ß2.1 + 15 I-19 edited ‚Äî see Decision log) |
| SQ-2 | 02 ¬ß7.2 pins `ComponentValues` as `map (MetricId, EpochId) ‚Üí SettledComponent` (no version), but 07 ¬ß2(4) runs the reporting game per `(component, epoch, frozen spec version)` with one settlement per version across a MetricSpec activation boundary ‚Äî the pair key cannot represent two per-version settled values. `pallet-oracle` (PR #30 follow-up) now keys rounds/settled values by the triple; the 02 contract row needs a resolution at the B2 mapping (append a versioned map or key change ‚Äî requires the joint backend+frontend sign-off and a contract-version bump per 02 ¬ß13). **The same B2 amendment batch also carries three more 02 reconciliations surfaced by the #46 Codex review (2026-07-15)** ‚Äî (a) the 02 ¬ß9 intake-row erratum (binding target for `intake.max_per_account` is `params()` + K-bound metadata constants, not a "‚â§ 4" metadata constant ‚Äî SQ-23); (b) splitting 02 ¬ß9's combined `epoch.length`/`epoch.slots`/phase-offset-fractions row so the fractions bind via metadata constants only (13 ¬ß3.1 resolution, SQ-24); (c) adding the `pallet-attestor` storage/view shape to 02 ¬ß7 (referenced by 01 ¬ß5.1 and 06 ¬ß7; read by the FE on the CODE/META execute path, SQ-26) ‚Äî so the contract version bumps once. | 02 ¬ß7.2; 07 ¬ß2(4) | 2026-07-14 | **oracle portion resolved 2026-07-15 (contract v3, see [SQ-58]);** the remaining three batch items ‚Äî (a) intake-row erratum (SQ-23), (b) phase-offset row split (SQ-24), (c) `pallet-attestor` 02 ¬ß7 row (SQ-26) ‚Äî still ride a **later** bump when their surfaces are next touched. The A5 ‚Üí B2 circular dependency forced the oracle key early; since pre-genesis bumps are free, "bump once" gives way to "bump per unblocked milestone" (this one was v2‚Üív3). |
| SQ-1 | E-row numbering collides: 15 ¬ß3.3 (self-described "normative row list") numbers its five decision-record additions E15‚ÄìE19 (E15 = VOID redemption ‚Ä¶ E19 = sudo banner + pinned-release warning), while 11 ¬ß11.12 ‚Äî which 15 ¬ß3 names as owner of the required-UX matrix ‚Äî defines E15‚ÄìE23 with different referents (E15 = referendum voting, E16 = VOID redemption, E21 = sudo era ‚Ä¶). Cross-refs disagree, e.g. 15 ¬ß4.8 "E15 redeem flow" = 11's E16. Which numbering is canonical? (Scenario content is equivalent; IDs conflict.) | 11 ¬ß11.12; 15 ¬ß3.3, ¬ß4.8 | 2026-07-12 | open |

## Verification log

`[VERIFY]` tags resolved against live sources (rule R-2), plus the standing backlog
lifted from the spec. Format: `| ID | Item | Spec ref | Status | Result |`

| ID | Item | Spec ref | Status | Result |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| V-1 | Re-verify all platform pins before implementation (SDK stable2603 umbrella, FE npm pins) | 01 ¬ß9 | complete (2026-07-13) | Re-verified via live web sources: GitHub releases list shows current stable2606 and stable2603 maintenance tags (stable2603-4), confirming `polkadot-stable2603` remains an extant release line; crates.io page/search confirms `polkadot-sdk` umbrella crate exists for the pinned crate family; npm package pages/search confirm the frontend packages and major lines in 01 ¬ß9 remain published (polkadot-api 2.x, smoldot 3.x, Vite 8, Dexie 4, plus the listed Arweave packages). Local direct `urllib`/`git ls-remote` verification was blocked by the container CONNECT proxy (403), so deeper API-level version enumeration remains a CI/network follow-up if exact patch drift matters before F0. |
| V-2 | Paseo onboarding / core allocation process | 01 ¬ß4.1 | open | |
| V-3 | Coretime renewal-price mechanics | 01 ¬ß4.1; 09 ¬ß4 | complete (2026-07-16, B4) | Verified via docs.polkadot.com (coretime-renewal + obtain-coretime), the Polkadot wiki (Agile Coretime) and `pallet_broker` rustdoc: bulk renewal = `broker.renew(core)` on the Coretime chain, **Signed origin, price-capped** ("rent-controlled") renewal charged at the sale's renewal price; the payer is whoever signs ‚Äî for a parachain that is its **funded account on the Coretime chain** (sovereign account; auto-renewal exists upstream, paritytech/polkadot-sdk#4424). B4's obligation is therefore exactly the 09 ¬ß4 funding leg: a DOT reserve transfer to the chain's renewal account there; no `Transact` needed for funding. Exact renewal-price *values* stay ops-verified at Phase-2/3 (V-12 family). |
| V-4 | Holds support on `pallet-assets` in stable2603 (else escrow fallback) | 03 ¬ß3 | open | |
| V-5 | Stock referenda/conviction-voting external per-poll voting-power provider (oracle track) | 06 ¬ß2.3 | open | |
| V-6 | XCM reserve-probe instruction sequence under Asset Hub barrier (no `Transact`) | 07 ¬ß8 | complete (2026-07-16, B4) | Verified against the live `asset-hub-polkadot` runtime `xcm_config.rs` (polkadot-fellows/runtimes, main): the AH barrier is `TrailingSetTopicAsId<DenyThenTry<DenyReserveTransferToRelayChain, (TakeWeightCredit, AllowKnownQueryResponses, WithComputedOrigin<(AllowTopLevelPaidExecutionFrom<Everything>, AllowExplicitUnpaidExecutionFrom<system>, ‚Ä¶)>, AllowSubscriptionsFrom<‚Ä¶>)>>` ‚Äî a **paid** sibling-parachain program `WithdrawAsset + BuyExecution + SetAppendix(ReportError) + DepositAsset` is admissible via `AllowTopLevelPaidExecutionFrom<Everything>`; **no `Transact` required in either direction** (the response returns as a `QueryResponse` our own barrier admits via `AllowKnownQueryResponses`). B4's `reserve_probe_program` builds exactly this shape and a unit test asserts no `Transact` recursively. |
| V-7 | Bulletin Chain mainnet availability (large evidence) | 07 ¬ß9 | open | |
| V-8 | `pallet-vesting` vs in-pallet schedule store | 08 ¬ß2 | complete (2026-07-16, B3) | **SDK `pallet-vesting` adopted**, pinned `=46.0.0` (the stable2603 family version per the `polkadot-sdk 2603.0.0` umbrella manifest, verified live on crates.io). Genesis semantics verified from the pinned source: `genesis_build` supports multiple schedules per account via `try_append` **but** installs the balances lock with per-entry `set_lock` (replace-not-accumulate), so a two-schedule cliff-catch-up composition would leave the cliff tranche under-locked (spendable) until the first `vest()` ‚Äî hence the conservative single-schedule curve pinned in 08 ¬ß2.1. 08 ¬ß2.1's [VERIFY] text replaced accordingly; `pallet-futarchy-treasury` keeps only its ¬ß1.3 USDC streams. |
| V-9 | smoldot/PAPI chainHead runtime-call verification semantics (FE-P2) | 02 ¬ß3; 10 ¬ß12 | open | Until resolved: cross-check runtime-API results vs storage reads on tx paths |
| V-10 | Two-pass Arweave deploy + ANT n-of-m capability (FE-P7) | 12 ¬ß1.2, ¬ß4.2 | open | |
| V-11 | FGP/SGF/GFP/EFP/AEGIS source-document identification (user/document owners) | 01 ¬ß9 | open | Blocks audit scoping only |
| V-12 | sim-gated defaults (`sec.prize.*`, `sec.flow_cap`, `collator.bond_req_vit`, `ops.*`, `fee.vit_usdc_rate_ref`) | 13 ¬ß1 | Phase-0 evidence produced 2026-07-18 (S4); publication parked (SQ-231) | S4 artifact `simulation/results/phase0-calibration.json`: sec.prize.param/code/meta 50k/300k/600k + sec.flow_cap 29 candidates-only ‚Äî eligibility red (PARAM/TREASURY decidable-harm false-pass 3.46 %/1.52 % ‚â• 1 %, sub-3P brackets dirty); CODE/META Œ¥ floors confirmed 0.000 %. `collator.bond_req_vit`/`ops.*`/`fee.vit_usdc_rate_ref` remain later-phase (Phase-2/3 ops, TGE). |
| V-13 | Multi-MB Wasm extrinsic submission via light client (FE-P10) | 11 ¬ß11.13 | open | Gates FE-15 upgrade-crank tier |
| V-15 | stable2603 **maintenance-pin adoption** forced by the B3 node closure (01 ¬ß9 regime) | 01 ¬ß9 | complete (2026-07-16, B3) | The node stack (`polkadot-omni-node-lib =0.14.1`, family version from the umbrella manifest) requires `polkadot-runtime-parachains 25.0.1`, which requires `staging-xcm-executor ^25.1.0` ‚Äî conflicting with the `=25.0.0` pin. Adopted `staging-xcm-executor =25.1.0` (+ transitive `polkadot-runtime-parachains 25.0.1`). Correlated to the **`polkadot-stable2603-1` maintenance release** (crates published 2026-04-15/2026-04-30 from its RC line; GitHub release tag published 2026-05-04; tags stable2603-1‚Ä¶-4 exist). Full gate suite re-run green after the bump, per the 01 ¬ß9 atomic-adoption procedure. Also vendored `core2 0.4.0` (every crates.io version yanked; required by `sc-network ‚Üí litep2p ‚Üí multihash`) as a byte-identical copy of the published package behind `[patch.crates-io]` ‚Äî provenance sha256 in `vendor/README.md`, independently re-verified by the spec-reviewer. |

| V-36 | **First real execution of the committed Zombienet environment (G1)** ‚Äî the B7 definitions had never been run end-to-end | 15 ¬ß4.7; 02 ¬ß11 | verified 2026-07-18 | Four executable-infrastructure facts established against the pinned stack: (1) the zndsl grammar accepts no `#` comments and requires `Creds: config` ‚Äî all 9 committed drills were unparseable as authored; repaired, all 9 now parse. (2) Every Zombienet timeout (toml `[settings] timeout`, DSL `within N seconds`) is 32-bit signed milliseconds ‚Äî values > 2,147,483 s silently overflow (the 6,000,000 s network timeout had collapsed to a 1 ms watchdog); drill 09's soak wait split into three ‚â§ 2,000,000 s steps. (3) substrate-wasm-builder resolves workspaces by walking up from `OUT_DIR`, so the Paseo-CSG build under an in-repo `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` ran `cargo metadata` over the Bleavit workspace and panicked (the release.yml dry-run failure, reproduced + fixed via `WASM_BUILD_WORKSPACE_HINT`). (4) paseo-local builds `ValidatorGroups` only at session boundaries (600-block sessions, `num_cores` genesis 0), so no injected para could ever be scheduled inside an assertion window ‚Äî fixed by the CSG `fast-runtime` build + `num_cores = 3` genesis seed; after the fixes drills 01/02/03 pass 10/10 against the real node/keeper binaries |
| V-37 | **ss58-7777 identities of the ten R-4 genesis-endowed protocol accounts** ‚Äî the chain-spec validator hardcodes these addresses, so they needed an independent derivation before the gate could be trusted | 03 ¬ß7 R-4; 02 ¬ß8 | verified 2026-07-21 | Re-derived from the substrate `AccountIdConversion for PalletId` rule (`b"modl"` + 8-byte `PalletId` + optional 8-byte sub-seed, zero-padded to 32) in **three independent implementations** ‚Äî mine, the deploy agent's, and the runtime's `r4_account_addresses_are_stable` test ‚Äî all agreeing byte-for-byte. Control: the three pot addresses already pinned in `PROTOCOL_POTS` (`treasury MAIN`, `community`, `incentives`) reproduce exactly from the same helper, which is what makes the ten new ones trustworthy rather than merely self-consistent |
| V-38 | **Serde shape of the USDC XCM `Location` in a genesis patch** ‚Äî the new chain-spec USDC gate compares the asset id structurally, so a wrong shape would reject valid specs | 02 ¬ß8 | verified 2026-07-21 | `staging-xcm 24.0.0` carries **zero** `#[serde(...)]` attributes anywhere in its `v5` module: `Location` and `Junctions` both use plain derives, so the encoding is externally tagged with unrenamed fields ‚Äî `{parents, interior: {X3: [{Parachain}, {PalletInstance}, {GeneralIndex}]}}`, exactly the shape pinned in `validate-chain-spec.py`. No generated dev spec exists in-tree to confirm against a real artifact, but the gate also cross-checks the pinned Location against the patch's own `foreignAssets.assets` USDC row, so any drift fails loudly rather than passing silently |
| V-39 | **`Preservation::Preserve` floor arithmetic in `pallet-assets 52.0.0`** ‚Äî the entire B14 failure analysis rests on what `Preserve` actually reserves | 03 ¬ß7 R-4 | verified 2026-07-21 | `functions.rs:247-274`: `untouchable = min_balance` when `keep_alive`, `Zero` when `Expendable`, so reducible is exactly `balance ‚àí min_balance`. The `is_sufficient` hypothesis was **refuted by execution** ‚Äî `can_decrease` never reads it; sufficiency only waives the consumer-reference requirement on account *creation*, never the withdrawal floor. Measured directly: leaving 3,000 ‚Üí `Err(Token(NotExpendable))`, leaving 10,000 ‚Üí `Ok`, and an `Expendable` drain ‚Üí `Ok` with residual 0. Genesis `accounts` semantics confirmed at `lib.rs:502/565/570` (mints, increases supply, requires ‚â• `min_balance`) |

## Decision log

Spec changes and other project decisions (rule R-1, AGENTS.md).

| Date | Amendment | Authorized by | Docs touched |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2026-07-21 | **Integration contract v6 (batch C) ‚Äî one bump, 32 rows disposed.** `INTEGRATION_CONTRACT_VERSION` 5 ‚Üí 6. **02 ¬ß6** gains a normative scope rule ‚Äî it freezes the events the canonical frontend ingests, is exhaustive for that ingest set, and is explicitly *not* an exhaustive declaration of every event a pallet may emit; an event MUST be listed iff the FE ingests it for an 11 workflow, another doc makes it normative, or it is the sole record of a terminal proposal/vault/cohort transition, and the shipped off-contract diagnostics are now named so the boundary cannot drift (resolves SQ-193's underlying question). Applying it: epoch gains `CohortVoided`, `BaselineCarried`, `SlotsShrunk` (`requested`/`funded` ruled to be proposal-slot counts, not USDC); the guard gains its five shipped lifecycle events with `PendingOutflowSyncFailed` named as ops-only; guardian gains all eight accountability variants; registry gains the two window events; a new attestor row freezes five names. **02 ¬ß2** appends branch-free `VaultState::BaselineSettled` (retiring a projection that fabricated `winner: Accept` for every Baseline position) and restructures `RatificationStatus` to `NotRequired`/`NoPassedRecord`/`Passed` ‚Äî `Pending` was unreachable by construction and `Failed { referendum: 0 }` rendered normal proposals as failed; `NoPassedRecord` is deliberately agnostic because the guard cannot prove the stronger fact. **02 ¬ß4** appends `ParamView.min_next`/`max_next`, `QuoteView.evaluable` and `WelfareView.active_spec_available`, and defines `ParamView`'s raw-scalar units, `escalated = round > 1`, and `decision_stats()` as a finalized view. **02 ¬ß7** corrects `Markets` to `MarketBook<AccountId>`, adds `CumulativeDeposits`, and rules unlisted raw storage outside the contract (SQ-284). **02 ¬ß9** drops the implementation-status column (R-4) and completes the freeze with 16 verified constants. **02 ¬ß12** makes guard ownership of offsets 112‚Äì119 and flag bit 2 normative, rules offset 112 the *currently installed* `spec_version`, adds the missing relay-abort occasion, and corrects the risk text: a writer could previously **suppress a live pending upgrade**, not merely raise a false banner. **02 ¬ß13** declares the contract version and SDK `transaction_version` independent counters. Fanout: 00 (v6 entry), 05 (T20 event name, cohort-VOID disposition, normative active-MetricSpec selector), 06 (ratify-track `attestor.resolve_challenge`, five attestor event names, `NoPassedRecord`, SQ-312 direction), 07 (registry window events), 08 ¬ß2.1 + 13 ¬ß3.5 (pot derivations frozen against post-B14 genesis), 09 ¬ß2.3 (writer-ownership statement reversed), 11, 12 + RB-RELEASE, 13 ¬ß5 (`MarketBook` 205 B; `CohortSummary` 81 ‚Üí 158 B; history total ‚Üí 61,700 B), 14 (TH-53 residual risk), 15 (I-6, I-16, **new I-30**; I-29 reserved for SQ-293). | User (owner for both backend and frontend sides under R-1), 2026-07-21, user-delegated batch ‚Äî 02 ¬ß13(1) joint sign-off. Pre-genesis revision, so ¬ß13(3)'s post-genesis append-only/migration clause does not bind; the one restructure is taken under that allowance as v3/v4 took theirs. | 00, 02, 05, 06, 07, 08, 09, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15; `deploy/runbooks/RB-RELEASE.md` |
| 2026-07-21 | **03 ¬ß7 R-4 trued and implemented (milestone B14) ‚Äî the endowment clause was specified but existed nowhere in code, and the obvious reading of its book-account half turned out to be wrong.** R-4 required the ledger sovereign, treasury sub-accounts and book accounts to be "genesis-endowed and can never be reaped"; nothing endowed anything, so the last full redeemer of the last open vault hit `Token(NotExpendable)` on a redemption 03 ¬ß5.3 makes legal. The rule is now written around its actual mechanism ‚Äî under `Preservation::Preserve` reducible balance is `balance ‚àí min_balance`, so an endowment of exactly `min_balance` is a permanent floor, and it binds **only** where every custody path out of the account preserves. That framing immediately exposed a second gap the original text hid: MAIN's one custody outflow used `Expendable`, which ignores the floor, so endowing MAIN alone would have changed nothing. **The book-account half was the substantive ruling.** Per-market accounts cannot be genesis-endowed (their addresses embed a runtime-allocated `MarketId`), and the tempting conclusion ‚Äî that R-4 therefore does not reach them ‚Äî is false: a Baseline book retains its sell-side fee as *plain* USDC, and below `min_balance` that retention is unpayable, so ordinary sells from `MinTrade` = 1 USDC up to ‚âà6.35 USDC were rejected. Verified by binary search against real custody (6,349,782 fails, 6,349,783 passes retaining exactly `min_balance`) rather than by reading. R-4 now distinguishes the three cases on whether the account custodies plain USDC at all, and names the registry sovereigns and epoch bond escrow as accounts that MUST stay un-endowed because their `Expendable` payouts exist precisely so draining the last bond stays admissible. 03 ¬ß1's "1-unit genesis endowment" was a straight internal contradiction (below `min_balance`, protects nothing) and is repaired. 04 ¬ß6.1 gains the Baseline sell-side degeneration that makes the carve-out legible; 08 ¬ß1.4 pins `fund_budget_line` to `MAIN ‚àí min_balance`. **No 02 surface touched ‚Äî no contract bump** (`INTEGRATION_CONTRACT_VERSION` stays 5).. **Corrected in-session after dual review, before merge:** the first draft of this amendment claimed the reaped Baseline residue is "recoverable only by TREASURY-class proposal under R-6" ‚Äî false on two counts (`recover_foreign` refuses `AssetKind::Usdc` outright and moves no custody, and R-6 is scoped to USDC arriving *outside* protocol flows), so the rule now states the residue is unrecoverable and names what implementing recovery would take. The bullet-2 MUST was also unqualified where a real seam violates it: the ledger `TREASURY` sub-account is XCM-addressable and the SDK `FungiblesAdapter` withdraws `Expendable` unconditionally. Finally the Baseline endowment is now normatively **best-effort and idempotent**, because making it a hard precondition of the seed propagates out of the epoch tick and reverts every proposal in the batch ‚Äî G-1 requires the narrow failure, not the broad one. Writing the spec first and the code second would have shipped all three; the reviews are what caught them. | Milestone B14 (user: "Implement milestone B14‚Ä¶ Decide on your own", 2026-07-21) | 03 ¬ß1, ¬ß7 R-4; 04 ¬ß6.1; 08 ¬ß1.4 |
| 2026-07-21 | **SQ-97's relaxation retracted before it could ship ‚Äî the second R-6 pass found the first repair still open.** Yesterday's repair made the for-cause revocation a mandatory MUST, which closed the adjudicated-liar scenario. Re-verification found the rule is **not implementable**: it keys on a departure "declaring cause", and 02 ¬ß7.5 freezes `AttestorInfo { account, bond, false_count, active }` with no cause field while the only removal call replaces the roster with a bare member list. `false_count` cannot cover every case ‚Äî `challenge_attestation` is deadline-barred once a 72 h window closes, so an attestor found unreliable afterwards can never be adjudicated false, leaving a cause-less recall as the values layer's only response. On that footing the relaxation is **strictly weaker** than the live-registry check it replaces, so 06 ¬ß7 and 09 ¬ß1.2(5) now state it is NOT in force and MUST NOT be implemented; the shipped roster read stands as the status quo, and so does the liveness trap SQ-97 opened with. The way out is **SQ-312** (batch C): either a cause-carrying removal surface in 02 ¬ß7.5 ‚Äî a contract change needing the v6 bump and joint sign-off ‚Äî or a narrower `false_count` predicate that needs no bump but redefines what a recall means. **Left to the user deliberately: one option is an integration-contract decision, the other changes a governance power's meaning.** Two premises of the first repair were also wrong and are fixed: challenge foreclosure after a departure comes from the SQ-248 held-bond lookup failing for an unseated attestor, not from any membership requirement on the challenger (¬ß7 makes challenging permissionless); and "outstanding attestations" now explicitly covers closed-window records, which are the load-bearing case. Also corrected: 09 ¬ß1.2(5) still said the admission conditions bind at *qualification* where 06 ¬ß7 and 09 ¬ß2.4 say queue time (~13 days apart); three vestiges of the superseded anchor design in 09 ¬ß3.2(2)'s lifetime text; RB-UPGRADE silently presented the unimplemented `started_at` stall predicate as live while dropping `ProgressMarker`, the key an operator actually needs to explain a raise ‚Äî it now carries the same "not yet implemented" caveat as the other gaps; and the two chopsticks cards plus README row 77 that still image the retired paired checkpoint. | Claude (user delegation, 2026-07-20); **SQ-312 explicitly escalated, not ruled** | 06 ¬ß7; 09 ¬ß1.2(5), ¬ß3.2(2); `deploy/runbooks/RB-UPGRADE.md`; `chopsticks/` |

the enforcement unit is the individual **numeric bound**, not the documentation row, with PB-LEDGER-FREEZE's I-4 drift-flag clause named as a gate rather than a bound (SQ-160); and a limit may be discharged by asserting the **specified boundary behaviour** where it enforces by clamp/no-op/latch/eviction instead of an error, exactly one binding per key (SQ-156). **15 ¬ß1's I-22 row was amended in lockstep** ‚Äî it read "asserts failure", which is false for every clamp-enforced limit and would have left the document contradicting its own ¬ß4.6. **15 ¬ß4.5** gained the advisory-source rule (RustSec **and** GHSA/OSV, partitioned so the two legs never duplicate, waivers expiring mechanically ‚Äî SQ-219), the exhaustiveness of the three fuzz areas plus permission to fuzz frame-free cores **provided** the production boundary carries its own hardening and a named runtime regression (SQ-226), and a weights block ratifying the committed-artifact diff gate *including its absolute noise floors and acknowledgement escape* (SQ-147), the pre-charge-and-refund model with its declared-inner-weight fallback (SQ-148), and worst-case heterogeneous batch weighing (SQ-149). **15 ¬ß4.9 and 09 ¬ß7.1** now state the same Phase-0 criterion: per class independently, **decidable-harm**, strictly below 1 % (SQ-269), with the nine-key calibration set resolved against 13's sim-gated tags so it cannot drift and publication explicitly distinguished from 13 adoption (SQ-268); the 1√ó profitability line is ratified as the exit threshold (SQ-273). **13** gained `gate.v_min` in reading rule 6 plus a corrected rule 7 (SQ-194), notes on rows carrying no seeded record (SQ-158) and on the unbounded sentinel (SQ-196), and a new ¬ß3.4 row for `UndecidingTimeout` (SQ-229). **12 ¬ß6.3** gained alert-condition semantics for the Upgrades and Liquidity-floors rows (SQ-265, SQ-266). *Corrections verification forced:* SQ-273's flip count (14 ‚Üí **62**) and its strategy label; SQ-269's dropped **decidable-harm** qualifier; SQ-227's whole premise (I-13 was already covered by an S1 property suite, so 15 ¬ß1's I-13 Verification column was corrected away from "fuzz" instead of a new obligation being invented); SQ-226's stale pin (stable2603 ‚Üí **stable2606**) and its "cannot link" (policy, not impossibility); SQ-157's `frn.window` example (it is 13 ¬ß3.4, and an alias of the covered ¬ß2 `dec.extension`); SQ-149's citations (`13 ¬ß5.1` does not exist; 09 ¬ß7.3 is Preimage discipline); SQ-229's premise (13 has no every-value-needs-a-row rule ‚Äî that is AGENTS.md, not spec ‚Äî and the value is not derived from 06 ¬ß2.1); SQ-265's union (`MIGRATION_FAILURE_HALT` is not an input); and SQ-268's timing premise (the producer had already frozen and passes). *Refusal (R-10):* **SQ-158** split ‚Äî `grd.bond` ratified as a kernel constant, but `dis.merit_min` reclassified to batch **X** as code, because ratifying the shipped hardcoded equality to `B_1` would silently delete the independent values-layer raise that both 07 ¬ß12 and 13 ¬ß1's governance metadata assert. *New finding riding the batch:* `UndecidingTimeout` equals the entrenched track's 7-day prepare period exactly ‚Äî verified benign under `pallet-referenda`'s `!in_queue` exemption, but now pinned in 13 ¬ß3.4 as the normative constraint that it MUST be at least the longest track prepare period. No 02 surface touched, so **no contract bump**; no D-n affected. **Dual review (native `spec-reviewer` 1 blocker/1 major/7 minor + adversarial Codex 2 blockers/3 majors) ‚Äî every finding fixed in-session, and the reviews earned their keep three times over.** The `spec-reviewer` blocker was self-inflicted and real: appending the SQ-194 rationale sentence to 13 reading rule 6 **broke the G0 Phase-0 gate**, whose extractor bound the class-suffix list with a greedy `$`-anchored `(.*)`, so the added prose was read as phantom suffixes and `tools/phase-gates/tests` went red. Fixed code-side ‚Äî the suffix list is now matched structurally so rule 6 may carry trailing prose ‚Äî plus a regression test. | 2026-07-20 | **B2 closure ‚Äî R-6 repair round: three blockers in the same day's own amendments, fixed.** The compliance review of the B2 rulings returned 22 deviations (3 blocker / 12 major / 7 minor). Recorded here because two of them were spec defects I introduced, not pre-existing ones. **(1) The SQ-97 ruling opened a hole.** As first written, the for-cause revocation clause was *conditional* ("if those attestations are to be invalidated") and never mentioned recall, while the dispatch check had already been relaxed ‚Äî so two attestors ejected for adjudicated-false attestations could still carry an upgrade to execution on records that can no longer be challenged, the 72 h window having closed. Strictly weaker than the roster read it replaced, and precisely the 14 TH-28 outcome. Rewritten as a **mandatory MUST** covering ejection and declared-cause removal, atomic with the departure, per-record event, explicitly load-bearing for the relaxation and forbidden to ship without it; plus a new rule that a departure must not foreclose an already-open challenge window (unseating an attestor currently makes their in-window attestations unchallengeable by anyone). **(2) The new try-state rule was unsatisfiable.** `PendingUpgrade.is_some() ‚áí anchor present` fails continuously on a healthy chain, because `PendingUpgrade` is written at `execute()` and the anchor at application ‚Äî the ‚â• 72 h `DescriptorLeadTime` gap the same bullet describes. Inverted to one-way in the other direction (*anchor present ‚áí latch ‚à® halt source ‚à® live cursor*), with an added clearing clause for the `type Migrations = ()` case where no completion callback ever fires and the anchor would otherwise leak. **(3) R-1 consistency was broken.** 15 I-19, 14 TH-28 and 11 ¬ß11.5 row 6 still stated the live-registry reading the ruling forbids; all three amended. The `ChallengeStatus` polarity fix had also been left as a remote reading-directive over five sites still written backwards (05 ¬ß1.3/¬ß2.1/¬ß5.5, 15 I-19, 09 ¬ß1.1(3)) ‚Äî those are now trued in place. Majors fixed with them: 09 ¬ß2.1(7)'s abort arm still cleared a checkpoint that cannot exist at abort time; ¬ß2.5's worked example and ¬ß9's Resolves row still described the retired formulation; 06 ¬ß7 named three different binding points for one check (bound uniformly to queue time, dropping a wrong T5 citation); 06 ¬ß5.4's repair claim was overstated (rule (ii) binds the failure test only, and repairs two of the ordering paragraph's three clauses, not all three ‚Äî the third is SQ-310); 09 ¬ß3.2(d)'s `max_steps ‚â§ MIGRATION_STALL_BLOCKS` had to become **strict**, since at equality this backstop fires before the SDK budget it is meant to sit behind; (d)'s stated harm was corrected from "opens the expedited lane" ‚Äî unreachable, by SQ-309's own argument ‚Äî to the false page and queue freeze. RB-UPGRADE: the reconstructed anchor was **off by one block** (the `UpgradeApplied` block *is* the last pre-migration block, not its parent), the trigger was still defined by the retired byte-equality predicate, and the repair procedure pointed at a lane that cannot be submitted while a cursor exists ‚Äî now carrying an explicit SQ-309 warning. **No contract bump**; 11 ¬ß11.5's change is the mechanical X-11i mirror of 09 ¬ß1.2(5), not an independent frontend decision. | Claude (user delegation, 2026-07-20: "Decide on your own what is best for Bleavit"); ratification requested | 05 ¬ß1.3/¬ß2.1/¬ß5.5; 06 ¬ß5.4, ¬ß7; 09 ¬ß1.1(3), ¬ß1.2(5), ¬ß2.1(7), ¬ß2.4, ¬ß2.5, ¬ß3.2(2), ¬ß3.2(d), ¬ß9; 11 ¬ß11.5; 14 TH-28; 15 I-19; `deploy/runbooks/RB-UPGRADE.md`; `chopsticks/README.md` |

every registered MBM must declare `max_steps() ‚â§ MIGRATION_STALL_BLOCKS` at integrity-test time. 13 ¬ß2's expedited-lane row restored to 09 ¬ß3.1's two arms. *SQ-45(c)* ‚Äî 06 ¬ß5.4: the review deadline is the **duration** the text states, not an epoch index; block-denominated is exact and until it ships the index comparison must be strict, because an under-run confiscates bonds for a review still legitimately in progress ‚Äî the G-1 test set by SQ-79/SQ-285. **One joint ruling.** *SQ-127 + SQ-144* ‚Äî 09 ¬ß3.2(2) rewritten: the PB-MIGRATION anchor is captured at code **application** (guard `on_initialize`, first block after the swap), is `(block_number, block_hash)` with the state root deleted, and is single-homed in a dedicated guard item rather than the queue row (¬ß1.2(13) deletes it first) or `UpgradeAuthorized` (02-frozen at two fields). try-state moves from equality to implication-plus-latch ‚Äî the equality is precisely what hid the vacuity, since both items were killed together one block before any migration could step. ¬ß3.2(2)'s "exactly the migration's writes" claim, false for any authorize-time capture given ‚â• 72 h of `DescriptorLeadTime`, becomes true. 09 ¬ß2.1(3)'s three-field `UpgradeAuthorized` trued down to 02 ¬ß6's frozen two-field spelling. **No ruling needed a contract bump**;
