| 2026-07-20 | **Spec-question cleanup pass ‚Äî four residuals ruled as shipped, one reclassified out of the backlog, and B1 closed.** Prompted by the user asking why the backlog was gaining rows: *"Why did you add new SQ questions instead of just reducing."* Fair challenge ‚Äî three of the four residuals raised over the previous two sessions were rulable under the standing delegated authority and should not have been filed. Re-examined and ruled: **SQ-285** (recovery-epoch withdrawal deferral) ratified ‚Äî the original reasoning misapplied G-1, which constrains what the protocol *does* on a failure path, whereas declining to process an exit is inaction that forfeits nothing; relaxing the guard would be the active choice. **SQ-286** ratified on both halves ‚Äî the freeze sweep covers `Queued` (force-rejection *is* the status quo, and the alternative needs maturity/grace extension machinery that exists only for the dead-man), and the freeze wins over a concurrent dead-man pause (a solvency anomaly dominates a liveness pause); 09 ¬ß1.2(10) is not left vacuous because the keeper sweep is per-proposal and bounded. **SQ-287** ruled for the code against the spec ‚Äî 06 ¬ß4(5)'s 100 % is now scoped to screening-stage *verified* findings, while a decide-time preimage mismatch carries rule 2's 10 %, decisively because 09 ¬ß7.3 pins the preimage at qualification so the proposer *cannot* unnote it. **SQ-288** reclassified to milestone **B14**: 03 ¬ß7 R-4 is unambiguous and the code simply does not implement it, so there was nothing to rule. Separately, B1's two deferred rows were closed after real verification: **SQ-188** ‚Äî guardian `force_rerun` sets the *same* durable `Proposal.rerun` flag as T13 despite never passing through the `Rerun` state, uniqueness is enforced producer-side, and the Œ¥+1 pp increment has exactly one consuming site and one consuming window; **SQ-187** ‚Äî the shared Baseline stores one window per distinct boundary triple with per-proposal ownership on top, so a rerun registers its own window, a sealed earlier window is consumed alongside it rather than blocking it, and no proposal can read another's boundaries. Both were under-documented, not defective. | User ("Cleanup pass", 2026-07-20), under the standing delegated ruling authority for the SQ backlog | 04 ¬ß8.4; 05 ¬ß4.8, ¬ß5.4; 06 ¬ß4 (new scoping paragraph), ¬ß5.3, ¬ß6.3 |
| 2026-07-20 | **SQ-92 + SQ-98 landed as code fixes ‚Äî the two B1-reclassified defects, plus the spec truing each exposed.** **(1) SQ-92 (audit scope A, stranded funds).** 03 ¬ß2.3/¬ß5 normatively require the epoch VOID to settle the epoch's Baseline vault at `s = 0.5`, and justify `BaselineState` having no `Voided` variant by it; `void_cohort` never did, so the vault stayed `Open` forever and single-sided holders (whose redemptions require `Settled`) could never redeem, invisibly to every solvency invariant because pair holders still exit at par via `merge_baseline`. Fixed by giving `WelfareOps` a `settle_baseline_void(epoch)` method ‚Äî welfare remains the sole settlement authority (05 ¬ß6); epoch may request a settlement but never choose its score ‚Äî with the score single-homed as the new kernel constant `futarchy_primitives::kernel::VOID_BASELINE_SCORE` and a `LedgerSettlement::baseline_open(epoch)` precondition making "no vault" and "already settled" silent no-ops (G-1) without swallowing genuine ledger errors. Wiring the settlement also **exposed a latent `pallet-market` defect**, fixed here: `observe_baseline_terminal` latched `SettlementObservedAt` without closing the book (its proposal counterpart does), while try-state requires every observed entry to be `Closed` ‚Äî unreachable before, because only the VOID path reaches a settlement with the Baseline book still open. A legal `void_cohort` would therefore have produced a try-state violation: the 03 ¬ß9 drift class that arms the I-4 flag and PB-LEDGER-FREEZE eligibility under D-9. Docs trued: 03 ¬ß5 gained the owning-transition paragraph (epoch-VOID path only; per-proposal `void(pid)` settles no Baseline) and 05 ¬ß7 gained item (5) separating "a VOID skips welfare *scoring*" from the one settlement it still performs ‚Äî the 03-vs-05 contradiction the row flagged. **(2) SQ-98 (crank-order race).** A ledger freeze produced two different ledger outcomes depending on which crank observed the proposal first: `tick` par-voided the vault, `decide_with` resolved it onto the Reject branch ‚Äî deciding whether Accept-branch holders recovered ¬Ω or nothing. `decide_with`'s freeze arm now shares `tick`'s exact predicate and force-rejects with a par void. Par void is also the only self-consistent choice: the freeze halts `market.crank_observe`, so the measurement path would await a score the freeze itself prevents producing. Docs trued: 06 ¬ß6.3's "fail decision-grade (staleness)" wording was replaced ‚Äî it implied `NotDecisionGrade`, which forfeits 10 % of bond under ¬ß4(3) and would fine proposers for an operational emergency ‚Äî and the precedence cost was documented normatively (the freeze test precedes the static guards, so a freeze forgoes the ¬ß4(5) slash; evaluating the guards first would reintroduce the divergence, and this was already race-dependent before the fix). 05 ¬ß2.1 T20 and ¬ß1.3 now name the freeze as a producer. **(3) Doc hygiene.** Eight references to doc 03 ¬ß7.4/¬ß7.5 ‚Äî sections that do not exist (¬ß7 is "Rounding, dust, fees, ED" with no subsections) ‚Äî were retargeted to ¬ß6.4/¬ß6.5; six were pre-existing, two were mine. Five stale refs in code comments were corrected the same way. **No 02 change and no contract bump:** `BaselineSettled` is an existing frozen event, newly reachable. **Residuals raised rather than ruled:** SQ-286 (should a freeze force-reject `Queued` items or let them wait out the bounded window per 09 ¬ß1.2(10); plus the unstated precedence between the freeze sweep and the dead-man hold) and SQ-287 (decide-time preimage mismatch slashes 10 % in code where 06 ¬ß4(5) says 100 % ‚Äî pre-existing and independent), and SQ-288 (03 ¬ß7 R-4 requires the ledger sovereign, treasury sub-accounts and book accounts to be genesis-endowed with USDC; `genesis.rs` sets `accounts: vec![]`, so the last full redeemer of the last open vault hits `Token(NotExpendable)` under `Preservation::Preserve` ‚Äî found while building the SQ-92 runtime fixtures). | User ("fixing SQ-92 and SQ-98 to fix batch B1"), 2026-07-20, under the standing delegated ruling authority for the SQ backlog | 03 ¬ß2.3/¬ß5; 05 ¬ß1.3, ¬ß2.1 T20, ¬ß7; 06 ¬ß6.3; `models/tla/proposal/Proposal.tla` (comment only) |
| 2026-07-20 | **SQ-129 ruled ‚Äî inflow caps bind before the mint; a cap refusal never traps locally.** Decided under the user's explicit delegation ("can you decide on your own what is best for the protocol Bleavit?") after batch B2 refused to ratify the row as a drill-routing question. **The defect:** 09 ¬ß5.2 promised a `deposit_cap`-refused depositor a self-scoped `claim_assets` recovery that 09 ¬ß6.1 and the runtime prove impossible ‚Äî an inbound message's trap is keyed under Asset Hub's origin, and `pallet_xcm.send`/`execute` are `Nobody` for every origin including sudo, so an ordinary over-cap Phase-3 deposit was permanently unrecoverable from this chain's side. **Ruling: enforce both caps before any local mint** (09 ¬ß5.2 amended; ¬ß6.1's mint-leg mitigation restated to cover both). The per-account check binds the beneficiary extracted from the inbound program ‚Äî ¬ß5.2's premise that the beneficiary is unknowable pre-mint holds only for the `AssetTransactor` interface, not for the message, which carries `DepositAsset{beneficiary}`. **Why not re-key the trap to the beneficiary (`SetAssetClaimer`):** verified incoherent for a *cumulative per-account* cap ‚Äî a recovery claim's deposit leg stays metered (`TrapRecoveryInflows::deposit_asset` ‚Üí `CappedInflows`, `runtime/bleavit-xcm/src/caps.rs:308-316`) by the very cap the beneficiary exceeded, and that cap never clears within the phase, so the holding re-traps on every attempt. **Why not document AH-governance-only recovery:** it would enshrine fund-stranding as intent, contradicting ¬ß6.1's own amendment rationale. G-1 requires a refusal to restore the status quo rather than manufacture a worse state; R-7 forbids the reading that strands a claim. Spec-ahead-of-code by design: the bleavit-xcm implementation is owed in batch X and the stranding path stays live until it lands, so SQ-129 remains open. No 02 surface touched ‚áí no contract bump. | Claude (user delegation, 2026-07-20: "decide on your own what is best for the protocol"); ratification requested | `docs/architecture/09-execution-upgrades-and-rollout.md` ¬ß5.2/¬ß6.1, PLAN.md |

## Unplanned changes

Repo changes outside any milestone (config tweaks, user-driven edits) ‚Äî one line each.

- 2026-07-12 ‚Äî Added `docs/design/claude-design-kit/` (user-requested): 7-file non-normative context pack for Claude Design (docs 10/11 copied verbatim with derived-copy headers; 00/01‚Äì09/13/14/15 distilled read-only) + `PROMPT.md`. Spec untouched; README/AGENTS repo maps updated.
- 2026-07-13 ‚Äî Project renamed "Bleevit" ‚Üí "Bleavit" (user-requested): literal replace across the same 10 living/derived files. `docs/architecture/` still contains zero occurrences, so no frozen-doc amendment was needed.
- 2026-07-17 ‚Äî Added `.github/dependabot.yml` (user-requested), then simplified it in a follow-up PR: monthly Cargo updates for the root workspace plus weekly Cargo updates for the keeper workspace and weekly GitHub Actions updates. Specification and milestone status untouched.
- 2026-07-17 ‚Äî Documented a `git rerere` recommendation in AGENTS.md R-9 (user-requested): recurring `PLAN.md` merge conflicts across concurrent PRs (every PR touches `Current focus`/`Milestones`/`Session log`) prompted the note to enable `rerere.enabled` locally so repeated conflict shapes auto-resolve. Local git config only ‚Äî nothing enforceable via a committed file; no spec or milestone status touched.
- 2026-07-17 ‚Äî Added a Cargo `ignore` block to `.github/dependabot.yml` for the polkadot-stable2603 family (`sp-*`, `frame-*`, `pallet-*`, `cumulus-*`, `staging-*`, `polkadot-*`, `parachains-common`, `substrate-wasm-builder`). The root workspace `=`-exact-pins that family deliberately (root `Cargo.toml` `[workspace.dependencies]` header: "so `cargo update` cannot silently drift the SDK mix"), so a per-crate bot bump fractures the release train and cannot compile; the train moves as a unit via a deliberate milestone, never a Dependabot PR. `keeper/` (separate workspace, caret ranges) stays auto-updated. Prompted by open PRs #81‚Äì85, each targeting a pinned key. No spec or milestone status touched.
- 2026-07-17 ‚Äî **PLAN.md table-structure gate added (user-requested: "the formatting for the tables in PLAN.md should never drift / break").** The trigger: a blank line inside the Track B milestones table had severed the B10/B11 rows from their header (rendering as raw pipe-text); the same defect orphaned V-25/V-30 in the Verification log and the last two Decision-log rows, and a sweep found three more GFM-broken rows (SQ-54/SQ-50 missing their `Raised` cell; unescaped `|` inside backtick spans in SQ-7/SQ-212 and two session-log rows ‚Äî GFM splits cells on pipes even inside code spans, only `\|` escapes). All fixed. Enforcement: `tools/ci/check-plan-tables.py` (strict GFM cell splitting; orphaned-row / missing-header / cell-count / stray-separator rules; fence-aware) + 11 unit tests in `tools/ci/tests/`, wired as a third Stop hook (`.claude/hooks/guard-plan-tables.sh`, blocks session end on a malformed table) and as a step in the `docs` CI job. No spec or milestone status touched.

| 2026-07-15 | A9 (PR #51 Codex-bot review round) | Addressed both `chatgpt-codex-connector` P2 comments on PR #51. **(1) Rolling issuance meter (resolves SQ-50 + SQ-54):** replaced the fixed-window `IssuanceMeter` (which reset `minted` at the 365-day boundary, letting a governance-timed `issue_vit` mint ‚âà2√ó `iss.inflation_cap` across the seam) with a bucketed **trailing 365-day `RollingMeter<366>`** ‚Äî the same primitive as the 30d/180d outflow meters. `issue_vit` rolls a COPY (G-1 atomic), caps against `bps(vit_supply ‚àí trailing_minted, limit_bps)` (supply at window start) and commits on success; the trailing sum is never reset, so seam-straddling mints are summed and cannot exceed the cap. `try_state` now checks the trailing sum against the **fixed kernel ceiling** `ISS_INFLATION_CAP_BPS` (2%, down-only) rather than the live cap, so a mid-window down-amendment can't trigger a release-blocking failure (SQ-54). | 2026-07-16 | A8+A11 (PR #56 ‚Äî Codex-connector review: 1√óP1 + 2√óP2, all TRUE, fixed) | Triaged the three `chatgpt-codex-connector` comments on PR #56 (A8 `pallet-epoch` + A11 `pallet-execution-guard`); **all three hold true against the spec, all fixed with regression tests.** **(P1 ‚Äî dead-man converted freezes into rejections, 05 ¬ß4.8)** the permissionless `tick` fed `process_hold = dead_man_engaged() ‚Äñ ledger_frozen() ‚Äñ stale` into `epoch-core::tick`, which **force-rejected/voided every non-terminal proposal** while the dead-man switch was engaged (ahead of the recovery no-op) ‚Äî the opposite of ¬ß4.8's freeze-queue / extend-windows / pause-clock, and outside 05 ¬ß2.1's T20 tick-force-reject triggers (VOID / stale-epoch only; dead-man‚ÜíReject is a *decide()*-time effect, 05 ¬ß5 step 2). **Fixed:** the T20 tick force-reject now fires only on `self.ledger_frozen ‚Äñ self.stale_process_hold(pid)`, while **dead-man FREEZES** the machine (`NoOp`, alongside `recovery_epoch.is_some()`). Cause: the workspace build writes **~35 GB** (`target/` 35G + keeper 1.7G measured locally) against a stock runner's ~14 GB free; earlier greens rode on rust-cache reducing writes plus runner-image variance. Fix: a plain-script "Free runner disk space" step (no third-party action, consistent with the pinning discipline ‚Äî drops dotnet/Android/ghc/ghcup/swift/CodeQL + docker images, ~30 GB) before the toolchain/cache steps of every full-workspace job (`ci.yml` `rust`, `release.yml` `gates` + `artifacts`), plus job-level `CARGO_INCREMENTAL: 0` (incremental artifacts roughly double target size for zero CI benefit; `build-runtime.sh` already exported it). Workflow-contract test pins the step on all three jobs. YAML parse + 59 release-tool tests green. | Push; re-watch the PR checks; if the freed ~44 GB still proves tight, split clippy/test into separate jobs or prune target between gate phases |

| 2026-07-18 | O5 (PR #106 ‚Äî Codex-connector review: 1√óP1 + 2√óP2, all TRUE, fixed) | All three findings were valid and fixed in a Codex fix round, orchestrator-verified. **P1:** `BleavitDescriptorLeadTimeUncovered` joined series from two different scrape jobs with a bare `and` ‚Äî Prometheus full-label-set vector matching made the result permanently empty, so the alert could never fire; fixed with set-op `and on()` matching, and every other rule expression was audited for the same cross-job hazard (none found ‚Äî the other cross-job combinators are `or` unions). **P2 (events):** classic `chain_subscribeFinalizedHeads` headers carry no hash, so the exporter resolved buffered notifications against the newest `chain_getFinalizedHead` and permanently skipped `System.Events` from intervening finalized blocks (missed `GuardianAction`/`UpgradeAuthorized`/`KeeperBudgetLow` counts); fixed by resolving each notified header's own number via `chain_getBlockHash` and processing the contiguous finalized gap per block, bounded by `MAX_EVENT_CATCH_UP_BLOCKS = 512` (a commented resource bound, not a protocol value) with over-cap skips counted into `bleavit_chain_scrape_errors_total` and logged, plus stale/repeat-notification idempotence. **P2 (keeper):** the inactivity rule embedded the 3600 s threshold in the expr AND kept `for: 1h`, paging at ~2 h instead of the spec's 1 h; the `for:` clause is dropped and the restart/disabled-role guard is now `last_successful_crank_timestamp_seconds > 0` in the expr (single delay; the never-succeeded-since-restart blind spot is documented in `deploy/monitoring/README.md` and covered by the self-monitoring meta-alerts + keeper/README's per-required-role mandate). Mechanical follow-through on 12 ¬ß6.3's byte-bound tables: `check-runbooks.py` taught comma-separated runbook annotations (+3 tests), RB-UPGRADE ‚Üí `severity: page` + frontmatter/index/escalation, RB-INTAKE/RB-KEEPER trigger cells, and the page-domain test now **derives** its expected set from doc 12 instead of a pinned literal. Derived docs re-trued (`deploy/monitoring/README.md`, `deploy/runbooks/README.md`, `keeper/README.md`, `tools/release/README.md`), including **seven stale `seam ‚Äî B10` labels** in the monitoring row map that B13 had already closed ‚Äî only the 3 O3 bootnode seams remain. **A self-review pass against the code caught four over-claims in the author's own first draft**, all corrected before review: 15 ¬ß4.5 named the fuzz workspace as separately audited (only root + keeper are); 15 ¬ß4.5 claimed mechanical stale-waiver expiry for both legs (only the GHSA leg has it ‚Äî the `.cargo/audit.toml` list rides the from-scratch re-triage); 12 ¬ß3.1 said an absent series \"alerts\" (absence-alerting is an operator meta-monitoring duty, explicitly not a ¬ß6.3 row); and 12 ¬ß6.3's trigger-cell rule as first written condemned the TWAP row's 96 % early-warning margin above `dec.coverage`, which is a monitoring choice rather than a restatement and is now carved out explicitly. Two changes were delegated to parallel subagents and both mutation-checked their tests; the genesis-seat validator leg is profile-gated after the agent found an ungated check would break the G1 drill spec pipeline. No runtime code touched. **An independent adversarial review then found three real defects in the amendments themselves**, all fixed before merge: 15 ¬ß5 claimed byte-for-byte validation stops a placeholder environment directory ‚Äî **empirically false**, since `artifact_hashes: {}` satisfies set-equality against an empty directory (guard added, mutation-checked); condition (3) of the tiering rule made `suites_skipped` normatively load-bearing while the paragraph beside it forbade consumers to depend on non-frozen fields (promoted to a frozen, validated field); and 12 ¬ß6.3's keeper bullet declared *the* blind spot when the shipped rule has **two** ‚Äî a `connected == 0` keeper is silenced entirely. Two review findings were accepted rather than fixed and are recorded honestly: the new spanning-runbook escalation MUST is review-enforced, not CI-enforced (a ¬ß6.3-domain‚Üí¬ß6.1-row mapping is not mechanically derivable without new frontmatter), and `verify-release` remains an unbuilt tool that doc 12 already binds in four other places (O1/F13). A pre-existing RB-BOOTNODE defect the review surfaced was also fixed: it told operators no Prometheus series name exists for the dial/retention probes when three are named in the rules ‚Äî they are unfed O3 seams, which is the operationally important fact. **A Codex PR review then raised two valid P2s, both fixed:** the new coretime-seat validator rejected only `None` and `TODO` while every *other* account field in the same function is format-checked through the existing `ss58_account_id` helper ‚Äî so a seated-but-malformed ceremony output (a non-SS58 authority, a 3-byte renewal account) would have cleared the release gate and failed only at genesis build; both seats are now decoded per their real genesis encoding (`AccountId` / `[u8; 32]`), with 6 malformed cases tested and both guards mutation-checked. And `tools/release/README.md` still documented `suites_skipped` as a consumer-tolerated extra after this session made it a required validated field, so an operator following the documented contract would have produced evidence that fails strict assembly ‚Äî the contract block and the frozen-field prose now match the validator, including the non-empty `artifact_hashes` rule. | 2026-07-21 | **B14** ‚Äî genesis endowment of the R-4 protocol accounts | Ten statically derived protocol accounts genesis-endowed with exactly `min_balance` USDC; `TreasuryPotFunding::fund` flipped `Expendable` ‚Üí `Preserve` so MAIN's floor actually binds. Regression proven both ways (`accounts: vec![]` restored ‚áí `Token(NotExpendable)`). **The milestone's open sub-question ‚Äî whether R-4 can bind book accounts ‚Äî was answered against the convenient reading.** Per-market accounts cannot be genesis-endowed, but a Baseline book *does* hold plain USDC (it retains the sell-side fee), and a binary search against real `pallet-assets` custody showed every Baseline sell from `MinTrade` = 1 USDC to ‚âà6.35 USDC was failing: 6,349,782 planck errors, 6,349,783 passes retaining exactly `min_balance`. Doubly masked ‚Äî `pallets/market/src/mock.rs` genesis-endows its shared BOOK account, and the in-memory differential oracle models no custody, so `market-core`'s own `minimum_trades_with_dust_fees_are_admissible` asserts the trade routes while production rejects it. Fixed by endowing the Baseline book at Seed from `POL_BASELINE` inside epoch `tick`'s storage layer; `Expendable` was evaluated and rejected because it folds the dust into the debit and sweeps the accrued fee into the sovereign where L-2 cannot see it. Spec trued per R-1 across 03 ¬ß1/¬ß7, 04 ¬ß6.1, 08 ¬ß1.4; no 02 change, no contract bump. Both reviewers independently disproved the amendment's R-6 recovery claim (`recover_foreign` refuses `Usdc`), and the blanket preserve-MUST is now qualified for the XCM-addressable `TREASURY` seam.
